Closes github/sweagentd#10708

## Problem

The committer identity (`git user.name` / `git user.email`) and co-author noreply email suffix were hardcoded to `github.com`. On Proxima/GHE data residency tenants (e.g., `acme.ghe.com`), the noreply domain is tenant-specific (`users.noreply.acme.ghe.com`), so:

1. The committer email did not match the system GPG signing key, causing signature verification failures
2. The co-author trailer used the wrong noreply domain, causing sweagentd server-side validation to reject commits

## Changes

- **`deriveGitHubHostname(serverUrl)`** — new exported helper that extracts the hostname from `GITHUB_SERVER_URL`, falling back to `github.com` if missing/unparseable
- **`deriveNoReplyEmailSuffix(hostname)`** — new exported helper that builds `@users.noreply.{hostname}`, mirroring the monolith's `stealth_email_host_name` logic (`lib/github/config.rb`)
- **`setGitConfig()`** — committer identity now set to `GitHub <noreply@{hostname}>` to match the system GPG signing key (was using `COPILOT_AGENT_COMMIT_LOGIN` / `COPILOT_AGENT_COMMIT_EMAIL`)
- **`commitChanges()`** — co-author trailer suffix derived from `GITHUB_SERVER_URL` instead of hardcoded `@users.noreply.github.com`
- Removed unused `demandGitHubUserEmail` import

## Examples

| Environment | `GITHUB_SERVER_URL` | Committer | Co-author suffix |
|---|---|---|---|
| dotcom | `https://github.com` | `GitHub <noreply@github.com>` | `@users.noreply.github.com` |
| Proxima | `https://acme.ghe.com` | `GitHub <noreply@acme.ghe.com>` | `@users.noreply.acme.ghe.com` |

## Follow-ups

- Server-side `parseCoAuthorTrailers` in sweagentd also needs to be parameterized (separate PR in github/sweagentd)
- CLI system prompt co-author trailer is also hardcoded: Allow shift+tab to dismiss autopilot confirmation dialog and cycle mode","body":"## Overview

### What

When the autopilot permission confirmation dialog is open, pressing shift+tab now dismisses the dialog (without committing any choice) and cycles to the next agent mode. Previously, the dialog blocked all shift+tab input, preventing mode cycling until the user interacted with the dialog.

If the user cycles back to autopilot after dismissing, the confirmation dialog appears again since no choice was made.

### Why

When cycling through agent modes with shift+tab (interactive → plan → autopilot → interactive), entering autopilot without all permissions enabled shows a confirmation dialog after a 500ms delay. This dialog replaced the input area entirely, breaking the ability to continue cycling modes with shift+tab. Users had to explicitly interact with the dialog before they could switch away, which was a friction point for users just tabbing through modes.

Extract InputContext from app.tsx (1/10)","body":"## Overview

### What

First step in a 10-PR series to decompose the monolithic `app.tsx` (5,200+ lines, 90+ `useState` hooks) into domain-specific React contexts.

Creates `InputContext` to manage 6 input-related state fields:
- `isCommandModeActive` / `slashCommandPickerIndex` / `submitCount`
- `locationKey` / `locationType` / `isExecutingShellDirectly`

**Diff: 2 files, +101/-9 lines**

### Why

`app.tsx` is the single hardest file to work in across the codebase. This series incrementally moves state into focused contexts to improve maintainability without a risky big-bang refactor.

InputContext is first because it has **zero dependencies** on other contexts — safest possible starting point.

### Design

- `InputProvider` wraps via `AppWithProviders` (the default export), so all existing tests and `interactiveMode.ts` work without modification
- Components can start consuming `useInputState()` directly in follow-up PRs
- For now, `app.tsx` destructures the context and uses the state as before

### PR Series
1. **→ InputContext** (this PR)
2. SessionContext
3. DialogContext + component updates
4. PermissionContext
5. UIContext + AppProviders
6. useSessionEvents + useSessionConfig hooks
7. useMCPSetup hook
8. usePermission*Setup hooks
9. useInputHandling hook
10. Cleanup + final validation

Add local semantic search over session history","body":"## Overview

### What

Adds local semantic search over session history — both compaction checkpoint summaries and raw user/assistant turn pairs from JSONL event files. This enables the agent (and user) to recall and search across all past sessions.

**New capabilities:**
- `search_sessions` tool: The LLM can search past sessions semantically, with optional filters (`cwd_contains`, `after`/`before` date range)
- `/recall <query>` slash command: Interactive search from the TUI
- `/reindex` slash command: Rebuild the search index from scratch (dev tool)
- Background indexing: New checkpoints are automatically indexed after compaction

**Architecture:**
- Local embeddings via `@huggingface/transformers` (all-MiniLM-L6-v2, 384-dim vectors, ~23MB ONNX model)
- Vector storage via `vectra` (filesystem-backed, in-memory index at `~/.copilot/search-index/`)
- Indexes checkpoint MD files (~42 from compacted sessions) AND JSONL turn pairs (~1,200 sessions including legacy flat `.jsonl` format)
- Parallel reindexing with bounded concurrency (8 sessions), serialized vectra writes to prevent index corruption
- Metadata filtering: `cwd` and `timestamp` as indexed fields for efficient date range queries

**New files:**
- `src/core/search/embeddingService.ts` — Lazy-loaded transformer embedding provider
- `src/core/search/checkpointParser.ts` — Checkpoint MD parser and discovery
- `src/core/search/jsonlExtractor.ts` — JSONL turn pair extraction (new + legacy formats)
- `src/core/search/searchIndex.ts` — Vectra-backed search index with parallel reindex
- `src/tools/searchSessions.ts` — `search_sessions` tool with rich LLM prompting
- `src/cli/commands/recallCommand.ts` — `/recall` slash command
- `src/cli/commands/reindexCommand.ts` — `/reindex` slash command with progress
- `src/core/workspace/backgroundIndexing.ts` — Post-compaction background indexing
- 27 tests across 3 test files

### Why

Session history accumulates but is currently opaque — there's no way to search across past work semantically. This is experimental infrastructure to explore how session context can be useful: "what did I work on last week?", "have I done something like this before?", "how did I handle X last time?". The tool description encourages the LLM to use it proactively for historical/temporal queries.

### Checklist

Add comprehensive telemetry to undo/rewind feature","body":"## Overview

### What

Add telemetry events across the entire undo/rewind lifecycle to understand usage patterns:

- `snapshot_created` — file counts, timing, deduplication stats per snapshot
- `snapshot_create_error` — snapshot creation failures
- `undo_confirmation_shown` — when the undo dialog appears
- `undo_confirmation_result` — confirmed vs cancelled outcomes
- `snapshot_rollback_error` — rollback failures
- `snapshot_unavailable` — undo attempted without git repo/commits
- Enhanced `session_snapshot_rewind` — restored file count, error count, snapshot indices, success, duration

### Why

The undo/rewind feature has minimal telemetry today (only `session_snapshot_rewind` with event ID and events removed count). We need comprehensive telemetry to understand how much the feature is being used, where users are dropping off, and what errors they encounter.

Defer autopilot permission dialog until first prompt","body":"## Overview

### What

Defers the autopilot permission confirmation dialog from mode-switch time (Shift+Tab) to first-prompt-submission time. The prompt is saved, the dialog shown, and on confirm the prompt is replayed automatically. On cancel the prompt text is restored to the input field.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/311f0f6c-1a7b-42a2-8837-afa0d08cd45e

### Why

Switching modes quickly via Shift+Tab was spammy, the permission dialog kept popping up even when the user was just cycling through modes without intending to use autopilot yet.

https://github.slack.com/archives/C0A8C7K5EM7/p1772119853129929?thread_ts=1772059529.000649&cid=C0A8C7K5EM7

add /init suppress subcommand","body":"## Overview

### What

Adds `/init suppress` subcommand that let users suppress the "No copilot instructions found" init suggestion on a per-repo basis. The suppressed folder list is stored in `suppress_init_folders` in the global config (`~/.copilot/config.json`). The init suggestion hook is gated on `configLoaded` so the suppression list is always available before the check runs.

### Why

Users who intentionally don't have repo-level copilot instructions (`.github/copilot-instructions.md` or `AGENTS.md`) see the init suggestion on every new session. There was no way to dismiss it permanently for a specific repo.

Add env loading indicator","body":"## Overview

Outcome of [slack conversation with design](https://github.slack.com/archives/C0A8C7K5EM7/p1770655348396209)
Relates to https://github.com/github/copilot-agent-runtime/issues/2831

Adds a "Loading env:" spinner indicator during CLI session startup that shows what resources are being loaded (instructions, agents, plugins, skills, MCP servers). Once all participants complete, a "Loaded env: ..." timeline entry is emitted.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/fb4edf99-b178-47d8-9946-cd599674d7e3


## Design decisions

- **Decoupled from React**: Participant functions are plain async functions; `useEffect` calls in `app.tsx` are one-liners
- **Priority ordering**: Instructions always appear first (`priority: -1`), other participants use default priority (`0`)
- **Replaces "Configured MCP servers" message**: MCP server count is now shown incrementally in the env loading indicator instead of a separate timeline entry after startup

Enable Content Exclusion FF for staff","body":"## Overview

### What

Enables the content exclusion feature flag for staff rollout, adds logging/telemetry instrumentation for observability, and fixes two bugs discovered during end-to-end testing.

**Bug fixes:**
- **`last_updated_at` schema mismatch**: The zod schema expected `last_updated_at` to be a number (matching the integration guide examples), but the API now returns an ISO 8601 date string. This caused zod validation to reject the entire response, resulting in 0 rules loaded and content exclusion silently not working. Fixed to accept both string and number via `z.union`.
- **Fail-closed on content rule file-read errors**: When a file cannot be read to evaluate content-based exclusion rules (`ifAnyMatch`/`ifNoneMatch`), we now block access instead of allowing it. This matches VS Code behavior — if we cannot verify whether content rules apply, we should protect the file rather than risk exposing excluded content. Added 2 tests covering this case.

**Feature flag:**
- Move `CONTENT_EXCLUSION` from `"off"` to `"staff"`

**Logging:**
- Add info/debug logging across the content exclusion pipeline:
  - Feature flag check (enabled/disabled)
  - Rule fetch results (count of rules and repos)
  - File blocks in str_replace_editor and shell tool (info level)
  - Grep/glob filter counts (debug level)
  - Submodule discovery, cache refresh, and error paths (debug/warning)

**Telemetry:**
- `content_exclusion_rules_fetched` event with `rule_count`, `repo_count`, `submodule_count`, `fetch_duration_ms` metrics — fires on both initial fetch and cache refresh (emitted in both interactive and `-p` modes)
- `content_exclusion_denied` property on tool denied results (flows through `tool_call_executed` events automatically)
- `content_exclusion_filtered_count` metric on grep/glob results when exclusion filtering removes matches

**Log level consistency:**
- Service-level `isExcluded` hit demoted to debug (tools already log at info)
- str_replace_editor block promoted to info (matches shell tool)

### Why

Preparing for staff rollout of content exclusion. We need observability into whether rules are being fetched, how many are active, and when files are being blocked, so we can monitor the feature in production before broader rollout. The `last_updated_at` bug was discovered during live testing against a real repository with content exclusion policies configured — content exclusion was completely non-functional due to the schema mismatch. The fail-closed fix addresses a safety gap where unreadable files with content rules were being allowed through.

Convert remaining callbacks to events (Core/CLI Decoupling PR 5)","body":"## Overview

### What

Convert remaining non-serializable callbacks in `SessionOptions` to event-based patterns, continuing the Core/CLI decoupling roadmap (PR 5 of 13).

**Fire-and-forget callbacks → session events:**
- `onFileCreated` → `session.info` event with `infoType: "file_created"`
- `onWarning` → `session.info` event with `infoType: "warning"`
- `onTaskComplete` → `session.task_complete` event (persistent, shows in timeline)

**Request/response callbacks → PendingRequestStore events:**
- `externalToolDispatcher` → `external_tool.requested`/`completed` events with `respondToExternalTool()` method
- `setQueuedCommandHandler` → `command.queued`/`completed` events with `respondToQueuedCommand()` method

**Not converted (with justification):**
- `setTelemetrySender` — Later on we'll move telemetry entirely into the runtime so the TUI doesn't deal with it at all. That's cleaner and simpler than changing it into an event subscription system.
- `onExitPlanMode` — Not in PR 5 scope per roadmap.

### Why

Part of the [Core/CLI decoupling roadmap](https://gist.github.com/SteveSandersonMS/3c69ffac690b5a122cbc9f6978e609c1). This PR removes callbacks that carry non-serializable data (like `AbortSignal`) or create tight coupling between core session logic and CLI/TUI code. After this, the remaining callbacks in `SessionOptions` are limited to telemetry and plan-mode exit, neither of which block the introduction of an RPC boundary.

Builds on PR 4 (#4021, now merged) which established the `PendingRequestStore` pattern for permission and user-input callbacks.

### Key design decisions

- **Abort handling preserved**: `session.abort()` now calls `pendingRequests.rejectAllExternalTools()` to immediately reject in-flight external tool calls, matching the old `AbortSignal`-per-invocation behavior.
- **Simplified `useAutopilotContinuation`**: Removed the idle gate (an artifact of the old callback pattern where task_complete had to be emitted on idle). The hook no longer subscribes to `session.task_complete` — it just auto-continues on `session.idle` in autopilot mode.
- **New event schemas**: `external_tool.requested/completed`, `command.queued/completed`, `session.telemetry` (all ephemeral).

### Checklist

- [x] The Overview section above is filled with a description of the PR
- [x] PR title is prefixed with a scope label
- [ ] PR link shared to [#copilot-agent-runtime-reviewers](https://github-grid.enterprise.slack.com/archives/C0A9X7S8MGX) Slack channel for review

Permission and user-input callback inversion (Core/CLI Decoupling PR 4)","body":"## Overview

Reviewer note: if you're scared by the `+1042` indicator, don't worry! The line count increase mainly comes from generated code (we now include permission requests in the event schema).

### What

Replace `requestPermission` and `requestUserInput` callbacks in `SessionOptions` with an event-based request/response flow (Core/CLI Decoupling PR 4, per the [decoupling roadmap](https://gist.github.com/SteveSandersonMS/3c69ffac690b5a122cbc9f6978e609c1)).

Instead of passing non-serializable callback functions into Session, the Session now:
- Emits `permission.requested` and `user_input.requested` **ephemeral events** when it needs approval or user input
- Exposes `respondToPermission(requestId, result)` and `respondToUserInput(requestId, response)` methods for clients to respond
- Uses a `PendingRequestStore` to correlate requests with responses via `requestId`
- Denies permission requests when no listener is subscribed (instead of silently skipping)

All four consumers (TUI app.tsx, promptMode.ts, SDK server.ts, ACP server.ts) now subscribe to these events instead of injecting callbacks.

**Key design decisions:**
- **Async-aware event dispatcher**: `emitInternal` detects thenable handler returns and catches rejections, so handlers can be plain async functions without IIFE/try-catch boilerplate
- **`PendingRequestStore`** (`src/core/pendingRequestStore.ts`): Pure emit-and-await store with no policy logic. Emits events, correlates responses via requestId using `Promise.withResolvers`
- **Policy in Session**: The deny-if-no-listener and skip-user-input-if-no-listener decisions live in `buildSettingsAndTools`, not in the store

**No user-facing behavior changes. No SDK API changes. No CCA changes.**

### Why

This is PR 4 of the [Core/CLI decoupling roadmap](https://gist.github.com/SteveSandersonMS/3c69ffac690b5a122cbc9f6978e609c1). PRs 1-3 are merged (#3811, #3946, #3948).

Non-serializable callbacks in SessionOptions prevent:
- SDK parity (callbacks can't cross an RPC boundary)
- Multiple UI clients per session
- Server/service deployment

Converting them to events makes Session's API fully serializable and enables future RPC-based architectures.

### Checklist
- [x] The Overview section above is filled with a description of the PR
- [x] PR title is prefixed with a scope label (e.g. `CLI:`, `CCA:`, `Runtime:`, `Chore:`, `SDK:`, or bracket-style `[CLI]`, `[CCA]`, etc.) to help others understand the scope of the change and potential impact
- [ ] PR link shared to [#copilot-agent-runtime-reviewers](https://github-grid.enterprise.slack.com/archives/C0A9X7S8MGX) Slack channel for review
- [ ] Check [#sweagentd-runtime-updates](https://github-grid.enterprise.slack.com/archives/sweagentd-runtime-updates) channel and merge the change to sweagentd once it is ready
- [x] (Recommended) Use feature flags to roll out changes progressively and reduce time to mitigation

Fleet Mode + SQLite-based todo tracking [Experimental]","body":"## Overview

### What

Adds a SQLite-based task tracking system to the Copilot CLI, gated behind an experimental feature flag. This provides structured task management with dependency tracking for complex multi-step operations.

**Key changes:**
- New `sql` tool for executing SQLite queries against a per-session database
- Pre-created `tasks` and `task_deps` tables for task tracking with dependencies
- "Ready tasks" surfaced in user prompt each turn (tasks with no pending dependencies)
- Timeline displays meaningful task operations ("Tasks: Created task: user-auth") instead of raw SQL
- Deprecates `update_todo` tool when SQL task tracking is enabled
- **New `/task` command** to view tasks with topological ordering (dependencies shown first)
- Gated behind `SQL_TASK_TRACKING` experimental feature flag

### Why

The existing `update_todo` tool is lightweight but does not support:
- Task dependencies and parallel execution coordination
- Queryable task state for sub-agents
- Rich task descriptions that persist across turns

This enables the agent to better coordinate complex work, especially when dispatching parallel sub-agents.

{"total_count":15,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":4092200579,"number":4969,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"Derive committer and co-author noreply emails from GITHUB_SERVER_URL","body":"## Summary

Fixes the hardcoded `@users.noreply.github.com` noreply email domain that breaks commit signing on Proxima/GHE data residency environments.

Closes github/sweagentd#10708

## Problem

The committer identity (`git user.name` / `git user.email`) and co-author noreply email suffix were hardcoded to `github.com`. On Proxima/GHE data residency tenants (e.g., `acme.ghe.com`), the noreply domain is tenant-specific (`users.noreply.acme.ghe.com`), so:

1. The committer email did not match the system GPG signing key, causing signature verification failures
2. The co-author trailer used the wrong noreply domain, causing sweagentd server-side validation to reject commits

## Changes

- **`deriveGitHubHostname(serverUrl)`** — new exported helper that extracts the hostname from `GITHUB_SERVER_URL`, falling back to `github.com` if missing/unparseable
- **`deriveNoReplyEmailSuffix(hostname)`** — new exported helper that builds `@users.noreply.{hostname}`, mirroring the monolith's `stealth_email_host_name` logic (`lib/github/config.rb`)
- **`setGitConfig()`** — committer identity now set to `GitHub <noreply@{hostname}>` to match the system GPG signing key (was using `COPILOT_AGENT_COMMIT_LOGIN` / `COPILOT_AGENT_COMMIT_EMAIL`)
- **`commitChanges()`** — co-author trailer suffix derived from `GITHUB_SERVER_URL` instead of hardcoded `@users.noreply.github.com`
- Removed unused `demandGitHubUserEmail` import

## Examples

| Environment | `GITHUB_SERVER_URL` | Committer | Co-author suffix |
|---|---|---|---|
| dotcom | `https://github.com` | `GitHub <noreply@github.com>` | `@users.noreply.github.com` |
| Proxima | `https://acme.ghe.com` | `GitHub <noreply@acme.ghe.com>` | `@users.noreply.acme.ghe.com` |

## Follow-ups

- Server-side `parseCoAuthorTrailers` in sweagentd also needs to be parameterized (separate PR in github/sweagentd)
- CLI system prompt co-author trailer is also hardcoded: {"total_count":2,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":3980383683,"number":3700,"state":"closed","locked":false,"title":"CLI: Add task_complete to timeline and stop agentic loop after the tool is called","body":"## Overview

### What

Wires up the existing `TaskCompleteTimelineEntryDisplay` component so it actually renders when the `task_complete` tool is called. Previously, the tool call timeline entries were explicitly suppressed (`return null` in `timelineDisplay.tsx`) and the dedicated `task_complete` timeline entry type was never pushed into the timeline. Now, when the agent calls `task_complete`, a green **✓ Task complete** message appears in the timeline.

Additionally, the agentic loop now terminates immediately when `task_complete` is called. `responseEmitted` is set to `true` in `chat-completion-client.ts` when the tool result for `task_complete` is processed, preventing any unnecessary continuation turns after the agent has signaled it is done.

### Why

The `TaskCompleteTimelineEntry` type, the `TaskCompleteTimelineEntryDisplay` component, and the suppression of the raw tool call entries were all set up — but the glue to actually push the timeline entry was missing. This was particularly noticeable after alt screen mode became the default, since the alt screen buffer is cleared on exit and the task complete indicator was never visible.

Stopping the loop on `task_complete` avoids a wasteful extra model call that would otherwise occur after the agent explicitly signals completion. This was also leading to errors.

Retrieve relevant user information from `/copilot_internal` endpoint, consolidate multiple API calls","body":"## Overview

### What

This pull request optimizes multiple API calls used to assemble user information from the GitHub `/copilot_internal` REST endpoint including:

- login name
- copilot plan
- copilot API URLs

This provides the benefit of a singular API endpoint to call without the problems with cached information including incorrect subscription-based API endpoint.

This information has been folded into the `AuthInfo` type in order to make it available wherever the type is plumbed.

This should not present any visible difference in experience aside from timing:

- switching users should clear model cache and present the user's model choices
- login as a new user should allow model retrieval and chat completion

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/55dbb2e5-e32a-43b7-9fb4-3dd310740df0

### Why

Fixes #2769

{"total_count":2,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":3980383683,"number":3700,"state":"closed","locked":false,"title":"CLI: Add task_complete to timeline and stop agentic loop after the tool is called","body":"## Overview

### What

Wires up the existing `TaskCompleteTimelineEntryDisplay` component so it actually renders when the `task_complete` tool is called. Previously, the tool call timeline entries were explicitly suppressed (`return null` in `timelineDisplay.tsx`) and the dedicated `task_complete` timeline entry type was never pushed into the timeline. Now, when the agent calls `task_complete`, a green **✓ Task complete** message appears in the timeline.

Additionally, the agentic loop now terminates immediately when `task_complete` is called. `responseEmitted` is set to `true` in `chat-completion-client.ts` when the tool result for `task_complete` is processed, preventing any unnecessary continuation turns after the agent has signaled it is done.

### Why

The `TaskCompleteTimelineEntry` type, the `TaskCompleteTimelineEntryDisplay` component, and the suppression of the raw tool call entries were all set up — but the glue to actually push the timeline entry was missing. This was particularly noticeable after alt screen mode became the default, since the alt screen buffer is cleared on exit and the task complete indicator was never visible.

Stopping the loop on `task_complete` avoids a wasteful extra model call that would otherwise occur after the agent explicitly signals completion. This was also leading to errors.

### Checklist

- [x] The Overview section above is filled with a description of the PR

Retrieve relevant user information from `/copilot_internal` endpoint, consolidate multiple API calls","body":"## Overview

### What

This pull request optimizes multiple API calls used to assemble user information from the GitHub `/copilot_internal` REST endpoint including:

- login name
- copilot plan
- copilot API URLs

This provides the benefit of a singular API endpoint to call without the problems with cached information including incorrect subscription-based API endpoint.

This information has been folded into the `AuthInfo` type in order to make it available wherever the type is plumbed.

This should not present any visible difference in experience aside from timing:

- switching users should clear model cache and present the user's model choices
- login as a new user should allow model retrieval and chat completion

{"total_count":1,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":3877130945,"number":2488,"state":"closed","locked":false,"title":"CLI: Add /research slash command for deep research capability","body":"## Overview

### What

Adds `/research <topic>` slash command for deep research capability using GitHub search, code exploration, and web search.

### Why

Closes #1999

### Demo

https://screen.studio/share/3BHCJ1wC?private-access=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJ2ZXJzaW9uIjoiMi4wIiwic2hhcmVhYmxlTGlua0lkIjoiMTBjNzU0NTItY2FkZi00N2FhLWJjZTgtOTQ1NWVkYWQ3N2JmIiwiaWF0IjoxNzcwOTQxMzM1fQ.oNbvF0L0tuFGIh1jZivRpX7ZyNh69JuHs_lwFlzHbpA

### Product Market Fit

* Sourcegraph [Deep Search](https://sourcegraph.com/deep-search)
* OpenAI [Deep Research Connector](https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/guides/deep-research) (uses GitHub's public code search API)
* Blackbird team heavy daily usage (using Blackbird to build/run Blackbird)
  * [SHA-256 MySQL Availability Incident Investigation](https://gist.github.com/gorzell/2dbe804501d80f38c3558cdcd5240855#executive-summary)
  * [How Messages Are Published to the cp1-iad.ingest.github.search.v0.RepositoryChanged Topic](https://github.com/github/azure-dotcom-environment/issues/170#issuecomment-3853457370)
  * [How does code search rate limiting work in the monolith? Do any secondary rate limits apply in Blackbird?](https://gist.github.com/terrhorn/58171aa274985a9456afea62b14c1c48)
  * [How Feature Flags are Implemented at GitHub](https://gist.github.com/gorzell/c2ae2584d450488d802772000bd2d962#how-feature-flags-are-implemented-at-github)
  * [Comprehensive Analysis: Agent Experimentation in GitHub Copilot Ecosystem - The Blackbird Tool](https://gist.github.com/krukow/bf3ca615ffdc1674f03e42ab7cc3bd25#comprehensive-analysis-agent-experimentation-in-github-copilot-ecosystem---the-blackbird-tool-1)
  
### Usage

```bash
# Research a topic
/research How does authentication work in this codebase?

# Share a research report  
/share research file    # Save to file
/share research gist    # Create GitHub gist
```

### Implementation

| Component | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| `research.agent.yaml` | Research agent with intent-adaptive prompt, explicit tool list |
| `RESEARCH_COMMAND` feature flag | Staff or Experimental-tier availability |
| `useResearchCapture` hook | Captures reports when session goes idle |
| `researchStorage.ts` | YAML front matter storage for reports |
| `researchPicker.tsx` | Two-step picker UI (report → destination) |

### Architecture Decision

We evaluated multi-agent (orchestrator → research sub-agent → explore agents) vs single-agent approaches. Single-agent with Opus was 9x faster (4 min vs 37 min) with better output quality and zero blocking interruptions.

<details>
<summary>Run Comparison Table</summary>

| Run | Architecture | Duration | Lines | Footnotes | Sections | Interruptions |
|-----|--------------|----------|-------|-----------|----------|---------------|
| **Final Opus (optimized)** | Opus → Tools | **4 min** | **451** | **47** | **13** | **0** |
| Opus Direct (initial) | Opus → Tools | 4 min | 519 | 44 | 10 | 0 |
| Single-Pass Sonnet | Opus → Sonnet (1 pass) | 9 min | 263 | 36 | 12 | 0 |
| 5-Pass Sonnet (v6) | Opus → Sonnet (5 passes) | 37 min | 392 | 71 | 10 | 2+ |
| 5-Pass Sonnet (v5) | Opus → Sonnet (5 passes) | 22 min | ~400 | ~45 | ~10 | 2+ |
| 3-Pass Sonnet (v3/v4) | Opus → Sonnet (3 passes) | 12-13 min | ~350 | ~35 | ~8 | 1+ |
| **Canonical (target)** | - | - | 499 | 47 | 13 | - |

**Key insights:**
- Multi-pass Sonnet runs took 5-9x longer than Opus Direct
- More passes ≠ better quality (5-pass had fewer sections than final Opus)
- Multi-agent runs had blocking interruptions (user input prompts, permission dialogs)
- Final Opus matches canonical benchmark exactly (13 sections, 47 footnotes)

</details>

### Testing

- 25 tests covering storage, capture hook, picker component, and slash commands
- Eval test validates output quality (citations, diagrams, tables, sections)

### Checklist

- [x] The Overview section above is filled with a description of the PR
- [x] PR title is prefixed with `CLI`, `CCA`, `Runtime`, or `Chore` to help others understand the scope of the change and potential impact

{"total_count":1,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":3877130945,"number":2488,"state":"closed","locked":false,"title":"CLI: Add /research slash command for deep research capability","body":"## Overview

### What

Adds `/research <topic>` slash command for deep research capability using GitHub search, code exploration, and web search.

### Why

Closes #1999

### Demo

https://screen.studio/share/3BHCJ1wC?private-access=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJ2ZXJzaW9uIjoiMi4wIiwic2hhcmVhYmxlTGlua0lkIjoiMTBjNzU0NTItY2FkZi00N2FhLWJjZTgtOTQ1NWVkYWQ3N2JmIiwiaWF0IjoxNzcwOTQxMzM1fQ.oNbvF0L0tuFGIh1jZivRpX7ZyNh69JuHs_lwFlzHbpA

### Product Market Fit

* Sourcegraph [Deep Search](https://sourcegraph.com/deep-search)
* OpenAI [Deep Research Connector](https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/guides/deep-research) (uses GitHub's public code search API)
* Blackbird team heavy daily usage (using Blackbird to build/run Blackbird)
  * [SHA-256 MySQL Availability Incident Investigation](https://gist.github.com/gorzell/2dbe804501d80f38c3558cdcd5240855#executive-summary)
  * [How Messages Are Published to the cp1-iad.ingest.github.search.v0.RepositoryChanged Topic](https://github.com/github/azure-dotcom-environment/issues/170#issuecomment-3853457370)
  * [How does code search rate limiting work in the monolith? Do any secondary rate limits apply in Blackbird?](https://gist.github.com/terrhorn/58171aa274985a9456afea62b14c1c48)
  * [How Feature Flags are Implemented at GitHub](https://gist.github.com/gorzell/c2ae2584d450488d802772000bd2d962#how-feature-flags-are-implemented-at-github)
  * [Comprehensive Analysis: Agent Experimentation in GitHub Copilot Ecosystem - The Blackbird Tool](https://gist.github.com/krukow/bf3ca615ffdc1674f03e42ab7cc3bd25#comprehensive-analysis-agent-experimentation-in-github-copilot-ecosystem---the-blackbird-tool-1)
  
### Usage

```bash
# Research a topic
/research How does authentication work in this codebase?

# Share a research report  
/share research file    # Save to file
/share research gist    # Create GitHub gist
```

### Implementation

| Component | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| `research.agent.yaml` | Research agent with intent-adaptive prompt, explicit tool list |
| `RESEARCH_COMMAND` feature flag | Staff or Experimental-tier availability |
| `useResearchCapture` hook | Captures reports when session goes idle |
| `researchStorage.ts` | YAML front matter storage for reports |
| `researchPicker.tsx` | Two-step picker UI (report → destination) |

### Architecture Decision

We evaluated multi-agent (orchestrator → research sub-agent → explore agents) vs single-agent approaches. Single-agent with Opus was 9x faster (4 min vs 37 min) with better output quality and zero blocking interruptions.

<details>
<summary>Run Comparison Table</summary>

| Run | Architecture | Duration | Lines | Footnotes | Sections | Interruptions |
|-----|--------------|----------|-------|-----------|----------|---------------|
| **Final Opus (optimized)** | Opus → Tools | **4 min** | **451** | **47** | **13** | **0** |
| Opus Direct (initial) | Opus → Tools | 4 min | 519 | 44 | 10 | 0 |
| Single-Pass Sonnet | Opus → Sonnet (1 pass) | 9 min | 263 | 36 | 12 | 0 |
| 5-Pass Sonnet (v6) | Opus → Sonnet (5 passes) | 37 min | 392 | 71 | 10 | 2+ |
| 5-Pass Sonnet (v5) | Opus → Sonnet (5 passes) | 22 min | ~400 | ~45 | ~10 | 2+ |
| 3-Pass Sonnet (v3/v4) | Opus → Sonnet (3 passes) | 12-13 min | ~350 | ~35 | ~8 | 1+ |
| **Canonical (target)** | - | - | 499 | 47 | 13 | - |

**Key insights:**
- Multi-pass Sonnet runs took 5-9x longer than Opus Direct
- More passes ≠ better quality (5-pass had fewer sections than final Opus)
- Multi-agent runs had blocking interruptions (user input prompts, permission dialogs)
- Final Opus matches canonical benchmark exactly (13 sections, 47 footnotes)

</details>

### Testing

- 25 tests covering storage, capture hook, picker component, and slash commands
- Eval test validates output quality (citations, diagrams, tables, sections)

### Checklist

- [x] The Overview section above is filled with a description of the PR
- [x] PR title is prefixed with `CLI`, `CCA`, `Runtime`, or `Chore` to help others understand the scope of the change and potential impact
- [x] PR link shared to [#coding-agent-runtime-reviewers](https://github-grid.enterprise.slack.com/archives/C0A9X7S8MGX) Slack channel for review
- [ ] Check [#sweagentd-runtime-updates](https://github-grid.enterprise.slack.com/archives/sweagentd-runtime-updates) channel and merge the change to sweagentd once it is ready
- [x] (Recommended) Use feature flags to roll out changes progressively and reduce time to mitigation

https://screen.studio/share/3BHCJ1wC?private-access=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJ2ZXJzaW9uIjoiMi4wIiwic2hhcmVhYmxlTGlua0lkIjoiMTBjNzU0NTItY2FkZi00N2FhLWJjZTgtOTQ1NWVkYWQ3N2JmIiwiaWF0IjoxNzcwOTQxMzM1fQ.oNbvF0L0tuFGIh1jZivRpX7ZyNh69JuHs_lwFlzHbpA

### Product Market Fit

* Sourcegraph [Deep Search](https://sourcegraph.com/deep-search)
* OpenAI [Deep Research Connector](https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/guides/deep-research) (uses GitHub's public code search API)
* Blackbird team heavy daily usage (using Blackbird to build/run Blackbird)
  * [SHA-256 MySQL Availability Incident Investigation](https://gist.github.com/gorzell/2dbe804501d80f38c3558cdcd5240855#executive-summary)
  * [How Messages Are Published to the cp1-iad.ingest.github.search.v0.RepositoryChanged Topic](https://github.com/github/azure-dotcom-environment/issues/170#issuecomment-3853457370)
  * [How does code search rate limiting work in the monolith? Do any secondary rate limits apply in Blackbird?](https://gist.github.com/terrhorn/58171aa274985a9456afea62b14c1c48)
  * [How Feature Flags are Implemented at GitHub](https://gist.github.com/gorzell/c2ae2584d450488d802772000bd2d962#how-feature-flags-are-implemented-at-github)
  * [Comprehensive Analysis: Agent Experimentation in GitHub Copilot Ecosystem - The Blackbird Tool](https://gist.github.com/krukow/bf3ca615ffdc1674f03e42ab7cc3bd25#comprehensive-analysis-agent-experimentation-in-github-copilot-ecosystem---the-blackbird-tool-1)
  
### Usage

```bash
# Research a topic
/research How does authentication work in this codebase?

# Share a research report  
/share research file    # Save to file
/share research gist    # Create GitHub gist
```

### Implementation

| Component | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| `research.agent.yaml` | Research agent with intent-adaptive prompt, explicit tool list |
| `RESEARCH_COMMAND` feature flag | Staff or Experimental-tier availability |
| `useResearchCapture` hook | Captures reports when session goes idle |
| `researchStorage.ts` | YAML front matter storage for reports |
| `researchPicker.tsx` | Two-step picker UI (report → destination) |

### Architecture Decision

We evaluated multi-agent (orchestrator → research sub-agent → explore agents) vs single-agent approaches. Single-agent with Opus was 9x faster (4 min vs 37 min) with better output quality and zero blocking interruptions.

<details>
<summary>Run Comparison Table</summary>

| Run | Architecture | Duration | Lines | Footnotes | Sections | Interruptions |
|-----|--------------|----------|-------|-----------|----------|---------------|
| **Final Opus (optimized)** | Opus → Tools | **4 min** | **451** | **47** | **13** | **0** |
| Opus Direct (initial) | Opus → Tools | 4 min | 519 | 44 | 10 | 0 |
| Single-Pass Sonnet | Opus → Sonnet (1 pass) | 9 min | 263 | 36 | 12 | 0 |
| 5-Pass Sonnet (v6) | Opus → Sonnet (5 passes) | 37 min | 392 | 71 | 10 | 2+ |
| 5-Pass Sonnet (v5) | Opus → Sonnet (5 passes) | 22 min | ~400 | ~45 | ~10 | 2+ |
| 3-Pass Sonnet (v3/v4) | Opus → Sonnet (3 passes) | 12-13 min | ~350 | ~35 | ~8 | 1+ |
| **Canonical (target)** | - | - | 499 | 47 | 13 | - |

**Key insights:**
- Multi-pass Sonnet runs took 5-9x longer than Opus Direct
- More passes ≠ better quality (5-pass had fewer sections than final Opus)
- Multi-agent runs had blocking interruptions (user input prompts, permission dialogs)
- Final Opus matches canonical benchmark exactly (13 sections, 47 footnotes)

</details>

### Testing

- 25 tests covering storage, capture hook, picker component, and slash commands
- Eval test validates output quality (citations, diagrams, tables, sections)

### Checklist

- [x] The Overview section above is filled with a description of the PR
- [x] PR title is prefixed with `CLI`, `CCA`, `Runtime`, or `Chore` to help others understand the scope of the change and potential impact
- [x] PR link shared to [#coding-agent-runtime-reviewers](https://github-grid.enterprise.slack.com/archives/C0A9X7S8MGX) Slack channel for review
- [ ] Check [#sweagentd-runtime-updates](https://github-grid.enterprise.slack.com/archives/sweagentd-runtime-updates) channel and merge the change to sweagentd once it is ready
- [x] (Recommended) Use feature flags to roll out changes progressively and reduce time to mitigation

{"total_count":47,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":3856686307,"number":2083,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"CLI: Add plugin installation scopes (user, project, local, managed)","body":"## Overview

### What

Adds plugin installation scopes matching Claude Code's functionality. Plugins can now be installed at `user` (default), `project`, `local`, or `managed` scopes with appropriate config file locations and merging behavior.

**Scope mappings:**
- `user` → `~/.copilot/config/config.json` (personal, cross-project)
- `project` → `.copilot/settings.json` (team shared, committed)
- `local` → `.copilot/settings.local.json` (project-specific, gitignored)
- `managed` → `managed-settings.json` (read-only)

**Config merging priority:** managed < user < project < local

**Usage:**
```bash
# Default user scope
/plugin install document-skills@anthropic-agent-skills

# Project scope for team plugins
/plugin install team-plugin@anthropic-agent-skills --scope project

# List shows scope for each plugin
/plugin list
# Output: • team-plugin@anthropic-agent-skills v1.0.0 [project]
```

**Implementation:**
- Added `PluginScope` type and `scope` field to `InstalledPlugin`
- Created `scopedConfig.ts` for multi-file config management
- Updated `installPlugin()`/`updatePlugin()` to accept optional scope parameter
- Modified `/plugin install` command to parse `--scope` flag
- Enhanced `/plugin list` to display scope indicators

**Backward compatibility:** Existing plugins without scope default to `user`. No breaking changes.

### Why

Enables personal plugins (user), team plugins (project), and local experimentation (local) without affecting team configuration.

### Checklist

- [x] The Overview section above is filled with a description of the PR
- [ ] PR title is prefixed with `CLI`, `CCA`, `Runtime`, or `Chore` to help others understand the scope of the change and potential impact
    - For changes that don't impact the CLI or are not user facing, apply the label `skip-changelog`
    - To have an automated changelog entry committed, apply the label `yolo-changelog`
- [ ] PR link shared to [#coding-agent-runtime-reviewers](https://github-grid.enterprise.slack.com/archives/C0A9X7S8MGX) Slack channel for review
- [ ] Check [#sweagentd-runtime-updates](https://github-grid.enterprise.slack.com/archives/sweagentd-runtime-updates) channel and merge the change to sweagentd once it is ready
- [ ] (Recommended) Use feature flags to roll out changes progressively and reduce time to mitigation

> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>Plugins: Support installation scopes (user, project, local)</issue_title>
> <issue_description>## Summary
> 
> Claude Code supports installing plugins at different scopes (`user`, `project`, `local`, `managed`), with `user` as the default. Copilot CLI does not currently support these scopes.
> 
> ## Expected Behavior
> 
> Copilot CLI should support the same plugin installation scopes as Claude Code:
> 
> | Scope | Settings file | Use case |
> |-------|--------------|----------|
> | `user` | `~/.claude/settings.json` | Personal plugins available across all projects (default) |
> | `project` | `.claude/settings.json` | Team plugins shared via version control |
> | `local` | `.claude/settings.local.json` | Project-specific plugins, gitignored |
> | `managed` | `managed-settings.json` | Managed plugins (read-only, update only) |
> 
> ## Current Behavior
> 
> Copilot CLI does not support installing plugins at different scopes.
> 
> ## Additional Context
> 
> From the [Claude Code plugins reference](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins-reference):
> 
> > **Plugin installation scopes**
> > When you install a plugin, you choose a **scope** that determines where the plugin is available and who else can use it.
> 
> Supporting scopes would enable:
> - Personal plugins that follow you across projects
> - Team-shared plugins committed to repositories
> - Local experimentation without affecting team configuration</issue_description>
> 
> ## Comments on the Issue (you are @copilot in this section)
> 
> <comments>
> </comments>
> 


</details>

### What

Users can now manage tool, path, and URL permissions without restarting the CLI:

- /allow-all-tools: Approve all tool calls for the rest of the session
- /allow-all-paths: Allow file access to any path for the rest of the session
- /allow-all-urls: Allow access to any URL for the rest of the session
- /reset-allowed-paths: Revoke session path permissions and return to initial directories
- /reset-allowed-urls: Revoke session URL permissions and return to initial URLs

Permission prompts now include an 'Allow all for session' option, letting users enable unrestricted access directly from the prompt instead of typing a command.

The /reset-allowed-tools command now properly resets the 'allow all' state, so tool calls will prompt for approval again after reset.

### Why

It's a feel bad when you forget to launch with `--allow-all-tools` or friends and then have to exit your session, restart and then use `--resume` or `--continue` to get back to your session.

"title":"CLI: Add in-app Copilot Free signup for eligible users","body":"## Overview

### What

Add in-app Copilot Free signup flow for eligible users who don't have a Copilot subscription. Instead of only showing a warning with a link to settings, the CLI now detects users eligible for Copilot Free (`can_signup_for_limited`) and presents an interactive prompt to sign up directly within the CLI.

**Changes:**
- New `subscribeCopilotFree()` API function that calls `/copilot_internal/subscribe_limited_user`
- New `refreshCopilotUser()` method on `AuthManager` to re-fetch subscription state after signup
- New `CopilotFreeSignup` component using the `Confirmation` pattern
- Integration into `app.tsx` at all subscription check points (initial load, login, device flow)

### Why

Users without a Copilot subscription currently see a warning and must navigate to a browser to sign up. This creates friction, especially for new users who are eligible for Copilot Free. By offering in-app signup, we reduce the steps needed to get started.

Relates to #2768.

### Checklist

- [x] The Overview section above is filled with a description of the PR
- [x] PR title is prefixed with `CLI`, `CCA`, `Runtime`, or `Chore` to help others understand the scope of the change and potential impact

- [ ] Check [#sweagentd-runtime-updates](https://github-grid.enterprise.slack.com/archives/sweagentd-runtime-updates) channel and merge the change to sweagentd once it is ready
- [ ] (Recommended) Use feature flags to roll out changes progressively and reduce time to mitigation

Typeahead buffer — capture keystrokes during CLI startup","body":"https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e4f11f52-29a1-4218-a504-f91458b7da64

## Typeahead Buffer for CLI Startup

### Problem
When the Copilot CLI starts, there's a multi-second delay while it loads config, authenticates, fetches feature flags, and boots the Ink/React UI. Any keystrokes typed during this window are lost — the user has to wait and retype.

### Solution
Add an early stdin buffer (`stdinBuffer.ts`) that captures keystrokes as soon as the process starts and replays them into the input field once the UI is ready.

### Key Design Decisions

**Env var gating (`COPILOT_TYPEAHEAD_BUFFER`):** The buffer is opt-in via environment variable. We considered using `config.experimental` or feature flags, but both require async loading (disk I/O at line 70 of index.ts, network for feature flags) — the buffer must start at line 15 before any async work. The env var is the only synchronous gating mechanism available. Any truthy value enables the buffer; `"0"` disables it.

**Buffer stays active through Kitty detection:** The buffer keeps its stdin listener attached during Kitty protocol detection. Both the buffer and Kitty detection receive stdin events (Node EventEmitter delivers to all listeners). Kitty protocol responses are escape sequences, which `processBufferedInput()` strips out — so they're harmlessly filtered. This maximizes the capture window.

**Flush right before React tree:** The buffer flushes immediately before `React.createElement(App, ...)`, capturing keystrokes typed during the entire startup sequence (auth, config, feature flags, Kitty detection, alt screen setup).

**Leaves stdin in raw mode after flush:** Restoring cooked mode would create a gap where keystrokes are line-buffered until Ink's `useInput` re-enables raw mode. Leaving raw mode active lets Ink take over seamlessly.

**10KB buffer cap:** Prevents memory exhaustion during slow startups. Buffer truncates incoming data to remaining capacity (never exceeds the cap).

**stdin pause on stop():** Non-interactive subcommands call `stop()`, which removes the listener, restores raw mode, and pauses stdin so the process exits cleanly.

### Escape Sequences Handled
CSI (`ESC [`), SS3 (`ESC O`), OSC (`ESC ]`), DCS (`ESC P`), APC (`ESC _`), SOS (`ESC X`), PM (`ESC ^`), plus Alt+key two-byte sequences.

### Testing
- 29 unit tests covering processBufferedInput (escape sequences, backspace, emoji surrogate pairs, unicode) and StdinBuffer (instantiation, no-ops)
- 2 e2e tests (basic typeahead + backspace processing)
- Full test suite (4048 tests) passing, lint clean

### Manual Testing
```bash
# Build
npm run build:cli

# Test typeahead (type immediately after running)
COPILOT_TYPEAHEAD_BUFFER=1 node dist-cli/index.js

# Test with artificial startup delay
COPILOT_TYPEAHEAD_BUFFER=1 COPILOT_DEBUG_STARTUP_DELAY_MS=3000 node dist-cli/index.js

# Verify disabled by default (keystrokes lost during startup)
node dist-cli/index.js
```

### Follow-up Work
- Mocked StdinBuffer lifecycle tests (start/flush/stop/Ctrl+C)
- Graduate from env var opt-in to default-on once validated
- Consider `StringDecoder` for multi-byte UTF-8 split across chunks (theoretical edge case)

CLI: Deep research workflow v1 (skill-based)","body":"## Overview

### What

This PR includes a first pass implementation of a deep research workflow for the CLI, using a skill and subagent approach.

### Why

The value of a research subagent was demonstrated by members of the Blackbird team, particularly focusing on cases that required gathering/synthesizing information from large repos, across multiple repos, and across remote repos using the GitHub mcp tools. This PR adapts the original slash command implementation to a more flexible skill -> subagent approach. 

The V0 implementation in this PR requires the setup of built in skills, similar to the functionality of built in subagents, in the CLI. Built in skills allow us to define workflows that we want Copilot to follow in particular cases. This is a functionality leveraged currently by [Claude Code](https://support.claude.com/en/articles/12512176-what-are-skills) for _"enhanced document creation for Excel, Word, PowerPoint, and PDF files."_

More information on the motivations behind a skill-based approach and planned future work/experimentation is available in the [deep research Epic](https://github.com/github/copilot/issues/26335) and [discussion/planning document](https://microsoft-my.sharepoint.com/:w:/r/personal/emilyrobles_microsoft_com/_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7B6BB48E12-6632-42F3-8998-F80BBB81AF8C%7D&file=Deep%20Research%C2%A0Discussion.docx&action=default&mobileredirect=true&DefaultItemOpen=1&wdOrigin=APPHOME-WEB.DIRECT%2CAPPHOME-WEB.FILEBROWSER.RECENT&wdPreviousSession=5a72e5ec-4bb0-4903-9afe-a9310b4f976a&wdPreviousSessionSrc=AppHomeWeb&ct=1770736147686&share=IQESjrRrMmbzQomY-Au7ga-MATsDxW9YMmMe72rXSWIE1qM).

Add xterm-based shell rendering behind XTERM_SHELL_RENDERING flag","body":"## Overview

### What

Adds a headless xterm terminal emulator (`@xterm/headless`) to process PTY output for shell tool results displayed in the CLI timeline. When the `XTERM_SHELL_RENDERING` feature flag is enabled, the shell tool's `sessionLog` (what the user sees in the timeline) and streaming partial output use the xterm screen state instead of raw `stripAnsi` text.

This only affects the **display** of shell output in the CLI — the model continues to receive the same raw text via `textResultForLlm`, so model behavior is unchanged.

The feature flag defaults to **off**. We want to get feedback from targeted folks before enabling at staff level.

Key changes:
- **TerminalScreen helper** (`src/helpers/terminalScreen.ts`): Wraps `@xterm/headless` with DSR response forwarding, scrollback reading, and async write support
- **InteractiveShellSession**: Creates a TerminalScreen and feeds PTY data through it, exposing screen state and flush methods
- **InteractiveShellToolContext**: Uses xterm screen state for `sessionLog` and streaming partial output when the flag is on
- **ShellTimelineEntry**: Prefers `detailedLog` (sessionLog) over `log` (textResultForLlm) for display
- **Bang commands** (`app.tsx`): Also use TerminalScreen when the flag is on
- **formatErrorOutput**: Split to return `baseOutput` and `suffix` separately so xterm screen state and timeout/exit messages compose correctly

### Why

Interactive CLI tools (like `azd`'s survey prompts) use cursor movement sequences (`ESC[1F`, `ESC[2K`) to redraw prompts in place. The existing `stripAnsi` path strips these sequences but preserves the text, causing duplicate/stacked frames in the timeline output. The xterm emulator processes these sequences correctly, collapsing redraws into a single final frame.

Contributes To: github/copilot-cli#1189

Add comprehensive telemetry to undo/rewind feature","body":"## Overview

### What

Add telemetry events across the entire undo/rewind lifecycle to understand usage patterns:

- `snapshot_created` — file counts, timing, deduplication stats per snapshot
- `snapshot_create_error` — snapshot creation failures
- `undo_confirmation_shown` — when the undo dialog appears
- `undo_confirmation_result` — confirmed vs cancelled outcomes
- `snapshot_rollback_error` — rollback failures
- `snapshot_unavailable` — undo attempted without git repo/commits
- Enhanced `session_snapshot_rewind` — restored file count, error count, snapshot indices, success, duration

### Why

The undo/rewind feature has minimal telemetry today (only `session_snapshot_rewind` with event ID and events removed count). We need comprehensive telemetry to understand how much the feature is being used, where users are dropping off, and what errors they encounter.

Support tilde in MCP server cwd and consolidate tilde expansion","body":"## Overview

### What

Consolidates all inline tilde (`~`) expansion logic into a single shared `expandHome()` helper in `src/helpers/path-helpers.ts`, and adds tilde expansion for MCP server `cwd` configuration.

**Commit 1 — Consolidate tilde expansion into shared `expandHome` helper:**
- Introduces `expandHome()` in `src/helpers/path-helpers.ts` that expands `~` and `~/path` while correctly ignoring `~username` patterns
- Replaces 5 duplicate inline implementations across `slashCommands.ts`, `directorySearch.ts`, `marketplace-loader.ts`, and `path-helpers.ts` itself (`getAbsolutePath`)
- Fixes `parsePluginInstallSpec` in `plugins/types.ts` to not misclassify `~username/repo` as a local path
- Fixes a latent bug in `getAbsolutePath` where `.replace("~", homedir())` could match `~` anywhere in the string

**Commit 2 — Expand `~` in MCP server `cwd`:**
- Adds `expandHome()` call in `ConfigResolver.resolveLocalServerConfig` so that `cwd: "~/projects"` in MCP server configs works as expected
- Tilde expansion runs after env var resolution, so `cwd: "$MY_DIR"` where `MY_DIR=~/projects` also works

### Why

- Multiple call sites had slightly different (and sometimes buggy) tilde expansion logic
- MCP server `cwd` was one remaining config field that didn't support `~` expansion, which users could reasonably expect to work

Fix hardcoded Github Repo name.","body":"## Overview

### What

Remove hardcoded repo name from the settings.

### Why

We are hardcoding the github repo name in the settings. This was discovered during the memory implementation, and this PR is a proper fix.

The hardcoded "copilot-sdk-session" repository name was introduced as a placeholder when the SDK was first created:

 - Sep 23, 2025 - Commit 4cd0e954c5f ("First stab at SDK") introduced
"sdk-session" as a placeholder value to satisfy the SettingsBuilder requirements
when creating the SDK's query functionality.
 - Oct 2, 2025 - Commit aebc290891d ("Add integrationId and hmacKey to Agent")
renamed it to "copilot-sdk-session" for consistency.

The placeholder was never intended to be a real value—it was just something to make
the settings builder work without a real GitHub repository context. However, this
causes issues for features that rely on the repository name, such as:

 - Memory service - memories get scoped to a fake repo, causing potential
collisions
 - GitHub Code Search (Blackbird) - searches against a non-existent repo
 - CodeQL - fails to download overlay databases
 - Telemetry - reports incorrect repository context

Add interactive review dialog for /research completion","body":"## What

Add an interactive review dialog that appears when the research agent finishes, similar to the `exit_plan_mode` flow. Extracts shared UI primitives from plan approval and builds `exit_research_mode` on top.

## Why

Currently the research agent just dumps output and tells users to press Ctrl+R. This adds a structured post-completion UX where users can:
1. **Done** — acknowledge findings
2. **Share to GitHub Gist** — one-click export
3. **Refine** — provide feedback for the agent to improve the research

## How

- Extract generic `ReviewPrompt` component and `useReviewQueue` hook from `PlanApprovalPrompt`
- Create `exit_research_mode` tool with done/share-gist/refine actions
- Wire through `ToolConfig` → `SessionOptions` → `app.tsx` callback/rendering
- Research agent YAML updated to call the tool after saving the report

## Key files
- `src/cli/components/reviewPrompt.tsx` — shared review dialog
- `src/tools/exit_research_mode.ts` — tool definition
- `src/cli/app.tsx` — callback wiring and gist sharing logic

## Testing
- 12 unit tests for exit_research_mode tool
- Snapshot updates for tool registration
- All existing plan approval tests still pass

Skip folder trust prompt when IDE lockfile has isTrusted set","body":"## Overview

### What

When an IDE lockfile contains `"isTrusted": true`, the CLI now skips the folder trust confirmation dialog at startup. This allows IDEs to signal that the workspace has already been trusted by the user, avoiding a redundant prompt.

   Changes:
   - Added optional `isTrusted` boolean to the lockfile schema (`LockFileInfoSchema`) and `IdeWorkspaceEntry` interface
   - Updated the folder trust check in `app.tsx` to look for a matching IDE lockfile with `isTrusted: true` before showing the dialog
   - Updated the mock IDE server to support `isTrusted` for testing
   - Added 4 unit tests covering `isTrusted` parsing (true, false, absent, multi-workspace propagation)
   - Added 1 e2e test verifying the trust dialog is skipped when `isTrusted: true` is present in the lockfile

### Why

When the CLI is launched from an IDE that has already established workspace trust, prompting the user again is unnecessary friction. IDE extension now writes `"isTrusted": true` in their lockfiles to communicate this, and the CLI should respect it.

Show subscription warning for Copilot Free users on /delegate, allow users to use keyboard shortcut to open links from timeline events","body":"## Overview

### What

Adds a proactive subscription check in the `/delegate` slash command that detects when a user has a `free_limited_copilot` plan. Instead of letting the user proceed through the delegation workflow only to hit a 403 Forbidden error, we now show a friendly timeline warning with a link to upgrade — and optionally open the upgrade URL in the browser.

Key changes:
- **Subscription check in `/delegate`**: Detects free-tier users and shows a timeline warning entry instead of proceeding to a 403 error
- **Event-related URL + browser open**: Introduces a mechanism for session events to carry an associated URL that can be opened in the browser, enabling hands-on-keyboard workflows
- **Input suppression support**: Adds `suppressInput` prop to `CustomTextInput` for leader-key-style interactions where character input should be blocked
- **Test coverage**: Adds unit tests for the subscription check, input suppression, timeline warning rendering, and an E2E test for leader-key suppression

<img width="1620" height="1055" alt="Screenshot 2026-03-11 at 2 20 05 PM" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9dbf9341-c291-4ebe-b9f6-0bf047c9b499" />


### Why

Copilot Free users hitting `/delegate` would go through the entire delegation workflow only to encounter a confusing 403 Forbidden error. This provides a clear, actionable message upfront with a direct upgrade link.

Fixes #1975

Spawn PowerShell with -nop for bang commands on Windows","body":"## Overview

### What

Spawns PowerShell with `-nop -nol` (NoProfile, NoLogo) for `!` (bang) commands on Windows, skipping profile loading. Also fixes the shell detection cache to use a shared promise so concurrent commands don't race.

Key changes in `src/cli/app.tsx`:

- **`-nop -nol` flags**: Spawns PowerShell with NoProfile/NoLogo for `!` commands, consistent with how agent-initiated shell operations already work in `interactiveShell.ts` and `hookCommandExecutor.ts`. On my machine with my relatively simple profile, this saves ~1.1s of profile loading overhead per command.
- **Promise-based cache**: Uses a shared promise (`cachedWindowsShellPromise`) to avoid repeated lookups for powershell.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3a7cc267-e13b-4098-b66b-d9db2dbdc8c4

### Why

User-initiated `!` commands were the only Windows PowerShell code path that still loaded the full user profile. The agent's own tool shell (`interactiveShell.ts`) and hook executor (`hookCommandExecutor.ts`) already launch PowerShell with `-nop -nol`. This change brings `!` commands in line, eliminating the ~1.1s profile loading overhead.

@shanselman, any concerns?

PoC: Persistent Background Agents","body":"## Overview

### What
- Implements **Persistent Background Agents** which are: 
  - (1) **Long lived**: they work continuously behind the main agent
  - (2) **Communicate many times with the main agent**: they continually provide assistance to the main agent, including fetching context from Issues/PRs, importing past session knowledge, implementing a GPT-based critic agent, etc. 
- Implements Persistent Background Agents for the following context sources -- they use haiku in the background to continuously stream relevant context to the Main Agent:
  - `Github-Context`: only has Github MCP tools, this works in the background observing user messages and sending relevant context to the main agent (see demo below)
  - `Session-Context`: only has SQL and bash tools, this works in the background observing user messages and finding relevant past sessions, including compaction summaries, change history, and relevant sessions.
  - `LSP-Context`: only has LSP tools, this continually does deep research via LSP tools

### Okay but why not just use `task`-based subagents?
- **Some subagents (like context-focused subagents) should long lived.** Subagents focused on _context_ IMO are especially suitable to being long lived because:
  - We know that search is quite noisy in the beginning. Early search calls, whether it's via Github MCP tools, LSP tools, SQL, or just classic grep/glob incurs an initial up front search which is rather noisy. With _persistent_ background agents, that up front search is incurred _once_.
  - Each user.message builds more and more context, which means the knowledge gathered by the background agent is increasing with every user.message

### What are some other advantages of Persistent Background Agents for context?
**Useful context is more easily preserved**
- PBA's message the Main Agent by posting to a Message Board, which is stored in the session folder. The Main Agent can choose to _read_ the message or ignore it. _At session end, all **read** messages are stored in the persistent `session-store.db`_ 
- The `Session-Context` agent can then simply retrieve past useful messages. 
**The user can explicitly direct the PBA's**
- If the user wants more control over what the PBA is searching, then can invoke the `tell` tool to directly steer the background agent to, e.g. focus more on change history when exploring past sessions
**PBA's can be used in `/fleet` mode**
- Per https://github.com/github/copilot/issues/26866 `/fleet` mode incurs context startup costs. PBA's can be rich context providers for the fleet of task-specific subagents.

### How do Persistent Background Agents solve this?
- I've configured a variety of Persistent Background Agents which are exclusively scoped to use the under-utilized tools. 
- The receive `user.messages` from the main agent so that they are always on-topic.
- They use their specific set of tools to explore the Users current questions, working quietly in the background without disturbing the main agent.
- When the background agents have messages to send to the main agent, they post these to a `MessageBoard` and the Main Agent can choose to call the `read_message` tool to read the full message content

### Demo showing `Github-Context` Persistent Background Agent:
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/af96ac09-916b-45c3-88f3-c74c61378d56



## New tools for Persistent Background Agent dynamics

### `create_agent` (main agent)

Creates a new persistent background agent and starts it working on an initial task.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `agent_type` | string | Yes | Agent type from available definitions |
| `prompt` | string | Yes | Brief initial prompt for the agent |
| `name` | string | No | Custom name (must be unique). Defaults to `{agent_type}-{counter}` |

**Behavior:**
1. Validates agent type (built-in or user-defined) and name uniqueness
2. Loads the agent definition and creates a context with tools, system prompt, and `send_message`
3. For noisy agents: attaches a callback to wake the main agent on message post
4. Registers the agent in the `PersistentAgentRegistry`
5. Starts the agent's tool-calling loop in the background
6. Returns immediately — the main agent is **not blocked**

### `tell` (main agent)

Sends instructions to a running persistent background agent. If the agent is busy, its current work is interrupted and replaced with the new task.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `to_agent` | string | Yes | Name of the target persistent background agent |
| `message` | string | Yes | Message or instructions to send |

**Behavior:**
1. Validates the agent exists (returns error with list of available agents if not)
2. If the agent is **Busy**: aborts current work via `AbortController`, discards partial results by restoring message history to pre-abort state
3. Delivers the message as a user message to the agent's persistent conversation history
4. Starts a new tool-calling loop in the background
5. Returns immediately — the main agent is **not blocked** — the main agent receives a `tell(type='receive')` notification when the agent completes

### `send_message` (PBAs only)

Posts a message to the Message Board. Only available to background agents.

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `message_name` | string | Yes | Short identifier (used as filename, no spaces) |
| `message_description` | string | Yes | Brief one-line description (shown on the board summary) |
| `message_body` | string | Yes | Full message content (written to `.md` file) |

When a noisy agent posts a message, the `onMessageSent` callback invokes `onPersistentAgentNotification()` to wake the main agent.

### `read_message` (main agent)

Reads the full body of a message from the Message Board. Content is returned to the agent (not shown to the user directly).

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `agent_name` | string | Yes | Name of the background agent that sent the message |
| `message_name` | string | Yes | Name of the message to read |

After reading, the message is marked as read on the board.

## Message Board

The Message Board is a file-based progressive-disclosure system:

```
{copilot_state_dir}/background_agents/
  message_board.txt              # Summary index (pipe-delimited)
  explorer-0/
    architecture-overview.md     # Full message body
    key-patterns.md
  reviewer-0/
    code-quality-findings.md
```

### Progressive Disclosure

1. **Board summary** is automatically injected into the main agent's context when PBAs post new messages
2. The main agent sees `agent | message_name | description | hasBeenRead` for each entry
3. The main agent **chooses** which messages to read via `read_message`
4. This keeps the main agent's context window lean — only relevant message bodies are loaded

### Injection

The `MessageBoardProcessor` (an `IPreRequestProcessor`) runs before each model call:
1. Checks for new message board entries since last injection
2. Injects a synthetic tool result showing the updated board
3. Guides the main agent to use `read_message` for relevant messages

### Receive Processor

The `PersistentAgentReceiveProcessor` handles delivery of PBA completions to the main agent:
1. Before each model call, checks for `pendingResponse` on any registered PBA
2. For each pending response, injects **two synthetic messages** into conversation history:
   - An **assistant message** with a tool call to `tell(type='receive', agent=name)`
   - A **tool result message** containing the PBA's response
3. This makes PBA completions appear as if the main agent had called `tell` and received a response

## Turn Forwarding

When the main agent completes a turn (user message + assistant response):

1. `forwardTurn()` checks each PBA's `interruptOn` configuration against the trigger type
2. Only PBAs whose `interruptOn` matches the current event are notified
3. For matching PBAs that are **Busy**: current work is **interrupted** (via `AbortController`), partial results are discarded by restoring `messages.length` to the pre-abort state
4. The turn content is forwarded as a user message
5. Message history is capped at 40 messages
6. The PBA can then:
   - React to the conversation context
   - Do additional exploration based on what's being discussed
   - Post new findings to the Message Board via `send_message`

### Interrupt Triggers

| Trigger | When Fired | Use Case |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `session.idle` | After main agent turn completes and session is idle | Background exploration, speculative prefetching |
| `user.message` | When a new user message arrives | Reactive context fetching, responding to new questions |

## Built-in Persistent Agent Types

| Agent | Purpose | Tools | Model | `interruptOn` | `noisily` |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **codebase-context** | Speculative codebase exploration — anticipates questions and pre-explores related systems | send_message, task | Haiku | `session.idle` | No |
| **github-context** | Fetches GitHub context — PR history, issue discussions, commit rationale | send_message, github-mcp-server/* | Haiku | `user.message` | Yes |
| **lsp-context** | Maps dependency graphs, call hierarchies, symbol usage, and interface implementations | send_message, lsp, grep, glob | Haiku | `user.message`, `session.idle` | Yes |
| **gpt-critic** | Observes conversation and surfaces alternative approaches from a different model's perspective | grep, glob, view, send_message, task | GPT-5.3-Codex | `session.idle` | No |
| **session-context** | Imports relevant knowledge from past sessions in the same repository and branch | sql, send_message, bash | Haiku | `user.message` | No |

add /babysit command for background PR monitoring (experimental)","body":"## `/babysit` — Background PR Monitoring

> **🧪 Experimental feature** — requires `/experimental` to enable.

### What

A new slash command that monitors a pull request in the background and automatically reacts to CI failures and review feedback.

### Usage

```
/babysit <pr-ref>              # Start monitoring a PR
/babysit list                  # List active babysit sessions
/babysit stop <session-id|all> # Stop monitoring
```

PR refs can be:
- Full URL: `https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/123`
- Short: `owner/repo#123`
- Number only: `#123` or `123` (when inside a repo)

### How it works

1. Polls PR state every 30s via GitHub REST API
2. Detects new CI failures (check runs with `failure`/`timed_out`/`cancelled` conclusions)
3. Detects new review comments and change-request reviews
4. On trigger, enqueues a prompt into the session with rich context (failed check names, review comment body, diff hunks, file paths) so the agent can investigate and push fixes
5. Automatically stops when the PR is merged or closed

### Architecture

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `src/cli/babysit/parsePrRef.ts` | Parse PR references in multiple formats |
| `src/cli/babysit/babysitGithubClient.ts` | GitHub API client for polling PR state, checks, reviews |
| `src/cli/babysit/babysitSessionManager.ts` | Core session manager — polling loop, trigger detection, lifecycle |
| `src/cli/babysit/index.ts` | Barrel exports |
| `src/cli/commands/babysitCommand.ts` | Slash command with `start`/`list`/`stop` subcommands |

### Feature flag

Gated behind `BABYSIT_COMMAND` at the `experimental` tier. Users must run `/experimental` to enable it.

### Testing

- [x] TypeScript compiles cleanly (`tsc --noEmit` passes)
- [x] Existing test call sites updated for new `babysitCommandEnabled` parameter
- [ ] Unit tests for parsePrRef
- [ ] Unit tests for BabysitSessionManager
- [ ] Integration / E2E testing

### Hackathon notes

This is a hackathon prototype. Known areas for future improvement:
- Add unit tests for the babysit modules
- Consider ETag/conditional request caching for API polling efficiency
- Add rate limit awareness beyond the existing `ghFetch` retry logic
- Consider persisting session state to survive CLI restarts
- Explore MCP server approach for plugin-compatible architecture

Replace Ctrl+X → / with Ctrl+P keyboard shortcut","body":"## Overview

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/cb7f63a3-00c2-4e66-b6df-04fbd8a6a515

| Header | Before | After |
|--------|--------|--------|
| Screenshot | <img width="8896" height="3054" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e2301fea-135b-482e-a24b-c0d888747be9" /> | <img width="8894" height="3054" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/babf7069-153c-4900-82ef-6067a55c07cd" /> | 

### What

Replaces the complex two-step `Ctrl+X → /` "leader mode" keyboard shortcut with a simplified single-keypress `Ctrl+P` to run slash commands while preserving input text.

**Changes**:
- Simplified keyboard shortcut activation from two-step chord to single keypress
- Uses Ctrl+P consistently across all platforms (Mac, Windows, Linux)
- Updated UI hints and help documentation
- Removed complex timeout and waiting state management

### Why

The original Ctrl+X → / implementation required users to press Ctrl+X, wait up to 2 seconds, then press / to activate command mode. This was:
- Hard to discover (non-standard UI pattern)
- Created timeout pressure
- Required complex state management
- More steps than necessary

The new Ctrl+P shortcut is simpler, instantly activated, and uses consistent behavior across all platforms.

**Before**: 
- Two-step chord: Ctrl+X → wait up to 2s → /
- Complex state: `isLeaderModeActive`, `isLeaderModeWaiting`, timeout ref
- 4 steps total with timeout pressure

**After**:
- Single keypress: Ctrl+P (consistent across all platforms)
- Simple state: `isCommandModeActive`, saved text ref
- 3 steps total, instant activation

**Net Result**: Code simplification with consistent cross-platform behavior

### Benefits

✅ Simpler UX (single keypress vs two-step)
✅ No timeout pressure  
✅ Consistent across all platforms (Mac, Windows, Linux)
✅ Code simplification
✅ Better discoverability

### Checklist

- [x] The Overview section above is filled with a description of the PR
- [x] PR title is prefixed with `CLI`, `CCA`, `Runtime`, or `Chore` to help others understand the scope of the change and potential impact
    - For changes that don't impact the CLI or are not user facing, apply the label `skip-changelog`
    - To have an automated changelog entry committed, apply the label `yolo-changelog`
- [ ] PR link shared to [#coding-agent-runtime-reviewers](https://github-grid.enterprise.slack.com/archives/C0A9X7S8MGX) Slack channel for review
- [ ] Check [#sweagentd-runtime-updates](https://github-grid.enterprise.slack.com/archives/sweagentd-runtime-updates) channel and merge the change to sweagentd once it is ready
- [ ] (Recommended) Use feature flags to roll out changes progressively and reduce time to mitigation

### What

### Why

This is done to enable E2E tracing between sweagentd and runtime

Skip [ctrl + e] custom behaviour when there is a written prompt","body":"## Overview

### What

* Timeline shortcuts (Ctrl+O, Ctrl+E) now only trigger when input field is empty making UNIX editing shortcuts take precedence when typing
* UI hint for "ctrl+e Expand all" now hidden when user is typing
* Updated help docs to clarify "(when no input)" and "(when typing)" behaviors

### Why

> Currently in UNIX Ctrl+e takes you to the end of the line. Well.. it just so happens that is a shortcut we use to "Expand recent" as well. So when I'm typing out some feedback for the agent and I do Ctrl+e, it both takes me to the end of the line and expands a whole bunch of stuff in the TUI
