Make paste tokenization configurable via config.json","body":"## Overview

### What

Made paste tokenization configurable through `~/.copilot/config.json` using a simple boolean flag. By default (`true`), pasted text with more than 10 lines continues to be tokenized as compact annotations, maintaining current behavior. Users can now disable this by setting `compact_paste` to `false` to see full paste content. The configuration option is documented in the CLI help system.

### Why

Users reported that pasting large bodies of text (>10 lines) into the CLI resulted in the content being hidden behind a compact token annotation, making it difficult to review and edit pasted content. The feature needed to be configurable to allow users who prefer to see full paste content to disable tokenization, while maintaining the current compact token display as the default behavior.

### Configuration

Users can disable compact paste display in `~/.copilot/config.json`:

```json
{
  "compact_paste": false
}
```

**Values:**
- Not set or `true` (default): Pastes with more than 10 lines are tokenized to `[Paste #N - X lines]` annotations (current behavior maintained)
- `false`: All paste content displays directly regardless of size

The configuration option is documented in the CLI help system and can be viewed with `copilot --help config`.

### Behavior

- **Default (no config or `true`)**: Pastes >10 lines are tokenized to `[Paste #N - X lines]` (maintains current behavior)
- **With `compact_paste: false`**: Large pastes display actual content
- Content scrolls if it exceeds maxLines (10 by default), showing last 10 lines
- All paste-related features (image detection, etc.) remain functional

### Implementation Details

**Configuration Schema:**
- Added `compact_paste` boolean field to `DefaultConfigSchema` in `config.ts`
- Defaults to `true` (tokenization enabled, maintaining current behavior)

**Paste Tokenization Hook:**
- Modified `usePasteTokenization.ts` to accept `compactPasteEnabled` boolean option
- Defaults to `true` to maintain current behavior
- Uses fixed threshold of 10 lines when feature is enabled
- Updated dependency array to include the boolean flag

**Component Updates:**
- Added `compactPasteEnabled` prop to `CustomTextInput` and `InputArea` components
- Passed `config.compact_paste` from `App.tsx` through the component tree

**Documentation:**
- Added `compact_paste` to the configuration help topic in `src/cli/index.ts`
- Documented default behavior (true) and behavior when disabled (false)

**Test Updates:**
- Added `compactPasteEnabled` prop to test Wrapper component
- Updated tests to explicitly pass `compactPasteEnabled={false}` to test non-tokenized behavior
- Adjusted assertions to account for terminal scrolling behavior when content exceeds visible lines

### Checklist

- [x] The Overview section above is filled with a description of the PR
- [x] PR title is prefixed with `CLI`, `CCA`, `Runtime`, or `Chore` to help others understand the scope of the change and potential impact
    - For changes that don't impact the CLI or are not user facing, apply the label `skip-changelog`
    - To have an automated changelog entry committed, apply the label `yolo-changelog`
- [ ] PR link shared to [#coding-agent-runtime](https://github-grid.enterprise.slack.com/archives/C099QKLD04U) Slack channel for review
- [ ] Check [#sweagentd-runtime-updates](https://github-grid.enterprise.slack.com/archives/sweagentd-runtime-updates) channel and merge the change to sweagentd once it is ready
- [ ] (Recommended) Use feature flags to roll out changes progressively and reduce time to mitigation

Unlimited reqs. label improvement & Unified TextSpinner TUIkit component with shimmer animation","body":"## Overview

### What

Replaces all spinner/shimmer implementations across the CLI with a single `TextSpinner` TUIkit component that supports three modes: icon-only, icon+label with shimmer, and label-only shimmer.

**Shimmer label for unlimited requests**: Shows an animated "Unlimited reqs." label at session start that auto-hides after the first message is sent. Provides a moment of delight for internal users without adding persistent UI noise.

**Unified spinner component**: Consolidates the old `SpinnerText` component and inline `<Spinner>` usage into `TextSpinner` with 6 semantic color variants (`default`, `brand`, `info`, `selected`, `secondary`, `placeholder`). 

**Accessibility**: Spinner icon is aria-hidden and shimmer animations disabled on screen-reader mode.

**Features replaced across**:
- **Thinking animation** — brand shimmer while the model responds
- **MCP server list/detail** — connecting spinners with `selected` variant when highlighted
- **Tasks dialog** — running task spinners with `selected`/`secondary` variants
- **Compaction indicator** — info variant shimmer during context compaction
- **Indexing indicator** — secondary variant while indexing the codebase
- **Upload debug logs** — secondary variant during log upload
- **Remote job delegation** — secondary variant for status checks
- **Quota info** — label-only shimmer for unlimited requests display

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/139ecac0-6f10-4784-a0c8-aeee1e58e070

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/689a7e2f-6f67-464a-a2fa-175fc55ad367


### Why

- The old `SpinnerText` was a one-off component with inline animation logic duplicated across callers
- The `∞` symbol for unlimited requests was unreadable — team feedback requested a better approach
- Hardcoded colors in spinner callers made theming inconsistent
- No support for finite animation loops or completion callbacks
- ThinkingAnimation Shimmer logic could be reused for other usecases

Add ctrl+g as both edit prompt and UI cancel","body":"## Overview

### What

Adds two orthogonal uses for ctrl+g

* Edit prompt in external editor (like ctrl+x ctrl+e)
* Dismiss/cancel in UI (like some but not all uses of escape)

### Why

Adding editor functionality to make it available on a single keypress and for Claude Code symmetry per Slack discussion.

Using ctrl+g for cancellation is an age old UNIX convention, sidestepping the inability to distinguish between the escape key and the meta/alt modifier. (These days we disambiguate with timing tricks, which are usually good enough.) This was a TODO for me way down on the priority list, but I am addressing it now because I want to make sure attempted dismissals of, say, the help dialog, don't unexpectedly dump the user into `vi`.

Add mid-session permission management commands","body":"## Overview

### What

Users can now manage tool, path, and URL permissions without restarting the CLI:

- /allow-all-tools: Approve all tool calls for the rest of the session
- /allow-all-paths: Allow file access to any path for the rest of the session
- /allow-all-urls: Allow access to any URL for the rest of the session
- /reset-allowed-paths: Revoke session path permissions and return to initial directories
- /reset-allowed-urls: Revoke session URL permissions and return to initial URLs

Permission prompts now include an 'Allow all for session' option, letting users enable unrestricted access directly from the prompt instead of typing a command.

The /reset-allowed-tools command now properly resets the 'allow all' state, so tool calls will prompt for approval again after reset.

### Why

It's a feel bad when you forget to launch with `--allow-all-tools` or friends and then have to exit your session, restart and then use `--resume` or `--continue` to get back to your session.

{"total_count":47,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":3856686307,"number":2083,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"CLI: Add plugin installation scopes (user, project, local, managed)","body":"## Overview

### What

Adds plugin installation scopes matching Claude Code's functionality. Plugins can now be installed at `user` (default), `project`, `local`, or `managed` scopes with appropriate config file locations and merging behavior.

**Scope mappings:**
- `user` → `~/.copilot/config/config.json` (personal, cross-project)
- `project` → `.copilot/settings.json` (team shared, committed)
- `local` → `.copilot/settings.local.json` (project-specific, gitignored)
- `managed` → `managed-settings.json` (read-only)

**Config merging priority:** managed < user < project < local

**Usage:**
```bash
# Default user scope
/plugin install document-skills@anthropic-agent-skills

# Project scope for team plugins
/plugin install team-plugin@anthropic-agent-skills --scope project

# List shows scope for each plugin
/plugin list
# Output: • team-plugin@anthropic-agent-skills v1.0.0 [project]
```

**Implementation:**
- Added `PluginScope` type and `scope` field to `InstalledPlugin`
- Created `scopedConfig.ts` for multi-file config management
- Updated `installPlugin()`/`updatePlugin()` to accept optional scope parameter
- Modified `/plugin install` command to parse `--scope` flag
- Enhanced `/plugin list` to display scope indicators

**Backward compatibility:** Existing plugins without scope default to `user`. No breaking changes.

### Why

Enables personal plugins (user), team plugins (project), and local experimentation (local) without affecting team configuration.

### Checklist

- [x] The Overview section above is filled with a description of the PR
- [ ] PR title is prefixed with `CLI`, `CCA`, `Runtime`, or `Chore` to help others understand the scope of the change and potential impact
    - For changes that don't impact the CLI or are not user facing, apply the label `skip-changelog`
    - To have an automated changelog entry committed, apply the label `yolo-changelog`
- [ ] PR link shared to [#coding-agent-runtime-reviewers](https://github-grid.enterprise.slack.com/archives/C0A9X7S8MGX) Slack channel for review
- [ ] Check [#sweagentd-runtime-updates](https://github-grid.enterprise.slack.com/archives/sweagentd-runtime-updates) channel and merge the change to sweagentd once it is ready
- [ ] (Recommended) Use feature flags to roll out changes progressively and reduce time to mitigation

> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>Plugins: Support installation scopes (user, project, local)</issue_title>
> <issue_description>## Summary
> 
> Claude Code supports installing plugins at different scopes (`user`, `project`, `local`, `managed`), with `user` as the default. Copilot CLI does not currently support these scopes.
> 
> ## Expected Behavior
> 
> Copilot CLI should support the same plugin installation scopes as Claude Code:
> 
> | Scope | Settings file | Use case |
> |-------|--------------|----------|
> | `user` | `~/.claude/settings.json` | Personal plugins available across all projects (default) |
> | `project` | `.claude/settings.json` | Team plugins shared via version control |
> | `local` | `.claude/settings.local.json` | Project-specific plugins, gitignored |
> | `managed` | `managed-settings.json` | Managed plugins (read-only, update only) |
> 
> ## Current Behavior
> 
> Copilot CLI does not support installing plugins at different scopes.
> 
> ## Additional Context
> 
> From the [Claude Code plugins reference](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins-reference):
> 
> > **Plugin installation scopes**
> > When you install a plugin, you choose a **scope** that determines where the plugin is available and who else can use it.
> 
> Supporting scopes would enable:
> - Personal plugins that follow you across projects
> - Team-shared plugins committed to repositories
> - Local experimentation without affecting team configuration</issue_description>
>

### What

Users can now manage tool, path, and URL permissions without restarting the CLI:

- /allow-all-tools: Approve all tool calls for the rest of the session
- /allow-all-paths: Allow file access to any path for the rest of the session
- /allow-all-urls: Allow access to any URL for the rest of the session
- /reset-allowed-paths: Revoke session path permissions and return to initial directories
- /reset-allowed-urls: Revoke session URL permissions and return to initial URLs

Permission prompts now include an 'Allow all for session' option, letting users enable unrestricted access directly from the prompt instead of typing a command.

The /reset-allowed-tools command now properly resets the 'allow all' state, so tool calls will prompt for approval again after reset.

### Why

It's a feel bad when you forget to launch with `--allow-all-tools` or friends and then have to exit your session, restart and then use `--resume` or `--continue` to get back to your session.

toolResult.sessionLog:
{"total_count":25,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":3958495962,"number":3450,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"CLI: Add performance measurement instrumentation with --perf flag","body":"## Overview

### What

Adds a comprehensive performance measurement system for the Copilot CLI, gated behind a hidden `--perf` flag. When enabled, it tracks rendering, event processing, perceived latency, startup, and memory metrics — printing a summary report to stderr on exit.

**New trackers:**
- **RenderPerformanceTracker** — frame count, render duration, CellRenderer diff stats, FPS
- **EventProcessingTracker** — debounce batch sizes, event-to-processing latency, queue depth
- **LatencyTracker** — time-to-first-token (TTFT), time-to-idle (TTI) per turn
- **StartupTracker** — CLI cold-start milestones (modules, session init, first render)
- **MemoryTracker** — labeled memory snapshots at lifecycle points
- **PerfReporter** — aggregates all summaries into a formatted terminal report

**Usage:** `copilot --perf` (combine with `--show-timing` for LLM turn timing too)

**Design:** Zero overhead when `--perf` is not passed — trackers are only instantiated conditionally. Instrumentation hooks into two chokepoints: `CellRenderer.handleFrame()` (all Ink frames) and `useTimeline` debounce pipeline (all session events).

### Why

The CLI has no systematic way to detect rendering regressions, diagnose UI bottlenecks, or quantify performance improvements. This lays the instrumentation foundation for developer diagnostics and future CI regression detection.

### Checklist

- [x] The Overview section above is filled with a description of the PR
- [x] PR title is prefixed with `CLI`, `CCA`, `Runtime`, or `Chore` to help others understand the scope of the change and potential impact

- [ ] Check [#sweagentd-runtime-updates](https://github-grid.enterprise.slack.com/archives/sweagentd-runtime-updates) channel and merge the change to sweagentd once it is ready
- [ ] (Recommended) Use feature flags to roll out changes progressively and reduce time to mitigation

### What

**New trackers:**

**Usage:** `copilot --perf` (combine with `--show-timing` for LLM turn timing too)

Support user-scoped .claude-plugin discovery","body":"## Overview

### What

Adds auto-discovery of `.claude-plugin` directories from `~/.claude/` to maintain compatibility with Claude Code's user-scoped plugin system. Plugins installed at user scope in Claude Code are now automatically available in Copilot CLI without manual reconfiguration.

### Why

Claude Code supports installing plugins at three scopes (user/project/local) per its [plugin reference](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins-reference), with `user` as the default. Copilot CLI previously only recognized explicitly installed plugins, requiring users to reconfigure plugins separately despite shared settings. This creates an inconsistent experience between the two tools.

### Changes

**Core discovery (`src/plugins/auto-discovery.ts`)**
- Scans `~/.claude/` for directories matching `*.claude-plugin` pattern
- Validates plugin structure (requires: skills/, .mcp.json, .github/mcp.json, plugin.json, or .github/plugin/plugin.json)
- Returns as `InstalledPlugin` entries with `marketplace: "user-scoped"`

**Integration**
- `src/cli/skills.ts`: Merges auto-discovered plugins into skill loading pipeline
- `src/cli/mcpConfigMerger.ts`: Includes auto-discovered MCP servers in config merge
- Priority: explicit installs → auto-discovered → additional config

**Testing**
- 9 new tests covering discovery, validation, multi-plugin scenarios
- All 65 existing plugin tests pass unchanged

### Checklist

- [x] The Overview section above is filled with a description of the PR
- [ ] PR title is prefixed with `CLI`, `CCA`, `Runtime`, or `Chore` to help others understand the scope of the change and potential impact
    - For changes that don't impact the CLI or are not user facing, apply the label `skip-changelog`
    - To have an automated changelog entry committed, apply the label `yolo-changelog`
- [ ] PR link shared to [#coding-agent-runtime-reviewers](https://github-grid.enterprise.slack.com/archives/C0A9X7S8MGX) Slack channel for review
- [ ] Check [#sweagentd-runtime-updates](https://github-grid.enterprise.slack.com/archives/sweagentd-runtime-updates) channel and merge the change to sweagentd once it is ready
- [ ] (Recommended) Use feature flags to roll out changes progressively and reduce time to mitigation

<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>Plugins: Support user-scoped .claude-plugin discovery</issue_title>
> <issue_description>## Summary
> 
> When a user installs a `.claude-plugin` directory at user scope in Claude Code, Copilot CLI does not detect or use it. This creates an inconsistency where plugins installed at user scope work in Claude Code but not in Copilot CLI.
> 
> ## Expected Behavior
> 
> Plugins installed at user scope (e.g., in `~/.claude/settings.json`) should be automatically available in Copilot CLI, just as they are in Claude Code.
> 
> ## Current Behavior
> 
> User-scoped plugins are not discovered by Copilot CLI. Users must manually reconfigure plugins for Copilot CLI even though other Claude Code settings are shared.
> 
> ## Additional Context
> 
> From the [Claude Code plugins reference](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins-reference):
> 
> > **Plugin installation scopes**
> > When you install a plugin, you choose a **scope** that determines where the plugin is available:
> > - `user` - Personal plugins available across all projects (default)
> > - `project` - Team plugins shared via version control
> > - `local` - Project-specific plugins, gitignored
> 
> Copilot CLI should respect user-scoped plugins to maintain consistency with Claude Code's behavior and reduce duplication of configuration.</issue_description>
> 
> ## Comments on the Issue (you are @copilot in this section)
> 
> <comments>
> </comments>
> 


</details>

alse,"title":"Red Team Mode: cross-model adversarial verification for agent responses","body":"## Summary

Adds **Red Team Mode** — a second model (the *critic*) evaluates every substantive agent response before it reaches the user. If it finds hallucinations, correctness issues, or safety problems, the primary model automatically retries with the critic's feedback. The user only sees the verified response.

**Key design choices:**
- Cross-family verification (Claude primary → GPT critic, and vice versa) — different families have different blind spots
- Per-turn, blocking — unlike Online Evaluation (post-session telemetry), this can trigger retries
- Graceful degradation — critic failure defaults to PASS, retry exhaustion passes through
- Read-only turn skipping — `grep`/`glob`/`View` turns are skipped to save latency

### What's included

| | Files |
|---|---|
| **Core** | `criticProcessor.ts`, `criticClient.ts`, `criticPrompts.ts` |
| **TUI** | `criticVerdictIndicator.tsx` |
| **Wiring** | 10 modified files (event types, pipeline, settings, CLI flags, slash commands) |
| **Tests** | 4 files (~1,700 lines) — unit tests + scenario test (Opus hallucinates `node:cache`, Gemini catches it) |
| **Docs** | `docs/red-team-mode.md` |

### How to test

```bash
copilot --red-team
copilot --red-team --red-team-model gemini-2.5-pro
```

```
/red-team on
/red-team status
```

:false,"title":"runtime: truncate mcp tools to 64 characters","body":"## Overview

### What

Truncate MCP tool names that exceed 64 characters to comply with model provider limits (e.g. OpenAI enforces a 64-character tool name limit). When the server namespace prefix + tool name exceeds the limit, the name is truncated with a user-facing warning. A `truncatedToolIdToOriginalName` map ensures truncated names are correctly resolved back to the original MCP tool name during invocation.

Changes:
- Add `MAX_TOOL_NAME_LENGTH` constant (64) in `mcp-registry.ts`
- Truncate tool names in all three MCP tool registration paths: `mcp-registry.ts` (in-proc), `mcp-transport.ts` (in-proc), and `agentPrimitives.ts` (out-of-proc)
- Emit a `session.warning` event so the CLI displays a one-time warning to the user when a tool name is truncated
- Add reverse mapping (`truncatedToolIdToOriginalName`) in `InProcMCPTransport` so truncated tool IDs resolve to the correct original name when invoking MCP tools
- Deduplicate the truncation warning so it only displays once per session (not on every turn)

### Why

Some model providers (notably OpenAI) enforce a 64-character limit on tool names. MCP tool names are prefixed with their server namespace (e.g. `server-name-tool-name`), which can push the total length beyond this limit, causing model API errors. This change gracefully truncates names while preserving the ability to invoke the original tool.

Add interactive review dialog for /research completion","body":"## What

Add an interactive review dialog that appears when the research agent finishes, similar to the `exit_plan_mode` flow. Extracts shared UI primitives from plan approval and builds `exit_research_mode` on top.

## Why

Currently the research agent just dumps output and tells users to press Ctrl+R. This adds a structured post-completion UX where users can:
1. **Done** — acknowledge findings
2. **Share to GitHub Gist** — one-click export
3. **Refine** — provide feedback for the agent to improve the research

## How

- Extract generic `ReviewPrompt` component and `useReviewQueue` hook from `PlanApprovalPrompt`
- Create `exit_research_mode` tool with done/share-gist/refine actions
- Wire through `ToolConfig` → `SessionOptions` → `app.tsx` callback/rendering
- Research agent YAML updated to call the tool after saving the report

## Key files
- `src/cli/components/reviewPrompt.tsx` — shared review dialog
- `src/tools/exit_research_mode.ts` — tool definition
- `src/cli/app.tsx` — callback wiring and gist sharing logic

## Testing
- 12 unit tests for exit_research_mode tool
- Snapshot updates for tool registration
- All existing plan approval tests still pass

> **Draft** — experimental, for side-by-side comparison with Fix shutdown race between ExitMessage render and process.exit()","body":"## Overview

### What

Fixes a race condition where `process.exit()` could fire before React renders the `ExitMessage` component, causing E2E tests to flake when expecting the "Resume" text after Ctrl+C exit.

### Why

The `shutdown` callback called `setShuttingDown()` (React state) and `shutdownService.shutdown()` (which calls `process.exit()`) in the same synchronous block. On slow CI machines, `process.exit()` would win before React got a chance to re-render with the exit message.

### How

Moved `shutdownService.shutdown()` into a `useEffect` triggered by the `shuttingDown` state. Since `useEffect` fires after React has painted, the `ExitMessage` (with "Resume" text and usage metrics) is guaranteed to be rendered before the process terminates.

### Testing

- `experimental-flag.test.ts` passes 5/5 runs locally
- Full E2E suite: 46 passed, 1 skipped, 1 pre-existing flake (model-picker)

Display diff in Edit tool output instead of generic message","body":"## Overview

### What

This PR changes the Edit tool's timeline display to show the actual diff instead of the generic "File path/to/file updated with changes" message when details are expanded.

This was accomplished by added an optional `displayContent` field to the `ToolExecutionCompleteEvent` schema that carries the detailed output (sessionLog/diff) for UI display, while keeping `content` for LLM chat messages (`textResultForLlm`). The CLI timeline now uses `displayContent` when available.

This separation ensures:
     - The model gets concise messages (`textResultForLlm`) for token efficiency
     - Users see detailed diffs (`sessionLog`) in the terminal

#### Before:

unexpanded:
<img width="144" height="42" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3bf27892-56e1-4d3d-a619-a483313c5771" />

expanded:
<img width="622" height="79" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c98f3dde-d676-42eb-9b9f-030bfb0310f7" />

#### After:

unexpanded:
<img width="194" height="35" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9f9efd99-e8f5-4de0-9e1a-8acadb27c398" />

expanded:
<img width="886" height="181" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/785db2c4-1289-4e21-b133-28067e5c46ce" />


### Why

This gives users more visibility into the changes that are being made. The diff was already being computed, but wasn't being surfaced in the timeline UI when details were expanded.



We used to have this behavior as of October 3 (in fact, the diffs automatically were expanded): https://github.com/github/sweagentd/pull/6825 -- I _think_ this was clobbered by the [timeline refactor that soon followed](https://github.com/github/sweagentd/pull/6960). 

I chose to only show the diff in the expanded view -- I could be convinced to always show it, though (perhaps behind a config option?)

### Checklist

- [x] PR title is prefixed with `CLI`, `CCA`, `Runtime`, or `Chore` to help others understand the scope of the change and potential impact

Add --yolo-fr flag for full autonomous fleet mode","body":"## Overview

### What

Adds a `--yolo-fr` ("YOLO for real") CLI flag that combines several autonomous execution features into a single switch:

- **Forces experimental features on** — enables all experimental feature flags
- **Forces autopilot mode** — auto-continues, auto-responds to `ask_user`, auto-handles permissions (both interactive and prompt modes)
- **Forces fleet mode** — prepends `FLEET_PROMPT` to every user message for parallel sub-agent execution
- **Implies `--yolo`** — enables all permissions (`--allow-all-tools`, `--allow-all-paths`, `--allow-all-urls`)

### Why

Running in full autonomous fleet mode currently requires combining multiple flags and slash commands (`--experimental`, `/fleet`, switching to autopilot mode, `--yolo`). This flag provides a single ergonomic shortcut.

### Changes

- `src/cli/commands/fleetCommand.ts` — Export `FLEET_PROMPT` constant
- `src/cli/index.ts` — Add `--yolo-fr` flag; `yoloFr` implies `yolo` and forces `isExperimental=true`
- `src/cli/interactiveMode.ts` — Wire `yoloFr` through to App component
- `src/cli/app.tsx` — Initialize `agentMode` to `"autopilot"` when `yoloFr`; prepend `FLEET_PROMPT` to messages
- `src/cli/promptMode.ts` — Prepend `FLEET_PROMPT` to prompt in both autopilot and single-turn sends

CLI rendering benchmark harness (Ink vs Alt-Screen vs OpenTUI)","body":"## Rendering Benchmark Comparison

Benchmarking harness + results comparing three CLI rendering implementations:
1. **Ink** (current baseline) — `node dist-cli/index.js`
2. **Alt-Screen** (PR #2881) — `node dist-cli/index.js --alt-screen`
3. **OpenTUI** (this branch) — `bun dist-experimental/index.js`

### Approach
- Single branch merging both `experimental/opentui-tui-rewrite` and `devm33/alt-screen-final`
- E2E tests as workload (replaying proxy, no real LLM auth needed)
- 8 test files (18 tests) selected for cross-variant compatibility
- 3 iterations per variant for statistical confidence
- Metrics: wall-clock time, peak RSS memory, pass/fail

### E2E Benchmark Results (3 iterations, 24 test runs per variant)

| Metric | Ink | Alt-Screen | OpenTUI |
|--------|-----|-----------|---------|
| Tests passed | **24/24** ✅ | **24/24** ✅ | **24/24** ✅ |
| Total suite time | 541.2s | **404.8s** 🏆 | 582.3s |
| Avg test time | 22.6s | **16.9s** 🏆 | 24.3s |
| Peak RSS | **442.7MB** | 476.4MB | 645.9MB |

### Per-Test Breakdown (avg across 3 iterations)

| Test | Ink | Alt-Screen | OpenTUI |
|------|-----|-----------|---------|
| tui-interactions (4) | 33.3s / 398MB | **24.9s** / 426MB | 50.4s / 529MB |
| cli-errors (4) | 55.6s / 433MB | **44.4s** / 476MB | 64.9s / 589MB |
| conversation-history (1) | 24.8s / 429MB | **8.0s** / 415MB | 10.7s / 555MB |
| grep-glob-tools (1) | 10.7s / 432MB | **6.2s** / 434MB | 11.1s / 646MB |
| intent-display (2) | 16.8s / 443MB | **11.4s** / 456MB | 13.7s / 546MB |
| agent-mode (2) | **19.7s** / 282MB | 28.6s / 282MB | 31.6s / 369MB |
| init-suggestion (3) | 15.6s / 272MB | **7.6s** / 281MB | **6.5s** / 358MB |
| clear-slash-command (1) | 3.9s / 423MB | **3.9s** / 430MB | 5.1s / 537MB |

### Key Findings

1. **Alt-Screen is consistently fastest** — 25% faster avg test time than Ink, with comparable memory
2. **OpenTUI uses ~46% more peak memory** (646MB vs 443MB) due to Bun runtime + OpenTUI overhead
3. **OpenTUI is ~8% slower** than Ink on average, and ~44% slower than Alt-Screen
4. **All 3 variants pass all 24 tests** — fair apples-to-apples comparison
5. **OpenTUI has xterm.js compatibility gaps** — help dialog overlays, streaming content truncation, and tool call rendering don't work in the xterm.js-based E2E harness (work fine in real terminals)

### Test Coverage

Tests were selected to cover diverse rendering behavior while ensuring all pass on all 3 variants:
- **UI rendering**: startup header, input area, cursor positioning
- **Error display**: permission denied, quota, model failure, rate limiting
- **Streaming**: intent display during streaming/non-streaming responses
- **Tool execution**: grep/glob tool output rendering
- **Multi-turn**: conversation history across exchanges
- **Mode switching**: agent mode cycling, plan mode
- **Commands**: /clear, init suggestion display

### Files Changed
- `benchmarks/rendering/scripts/e2e-subset.sh` — Updated test suite for cross-variant compatibility
- `benchmarks/rendering/` — Full benchmark harness (scripts, wrappers, README)
- `src/cli/experimental/` — Minor OpenTUI E2E compatibility fixes (placeholder text, prompt spacing, --disable-remote-sessions flag)

ShimmerLabel TUIkit component + suggestion hint UX","body":"## TL;DR

Extracts the shimmer animation into a reusable `ShimmerLabel` TUIkit component and improves the suggest-next-step UX with a dedicated hint bar and Esc-to-dismiss.

### Changes

- **`ShimmerLabel` TUIkit component** — two variants: `brand` (thinking animation) and `placeholder` (ghost suggestions, with ANSI dim)
- **Thinking animation** — replaced inline `ShimmerText` with `<ShimmerLabel variant="brand" />`
- **Suggestion ghost text** — replaced inline `SuggestionShimmer` + typewriter animation with `<ShimmerLabel variant="placeholder" />`
- **Hint bar** — when a suggestion is active, all hints are replaced with a single `tab accept suggestion` hint
- **Esc to dismiss** — pressing Esc clears the suggestion and returns to normal state (undiscoverable, no hint shown)
- **Removed** — typewriter animation state from app.tsx, `inlineSuggestionDisplayText` prop (replaced by `isAgentSuggestion` boolean)

### Files
| File | What |
|------|------|
| `src/cli/tuikit/components/ShimmerLabel.tsx` | New component |
| `test/cli/tuikit/components/ShimmerLabel.test.tsx` | 8 tests |
| `src/cli/tuikit/index.ts` | Export |
| `src/cli/tuikit/README.md` | Docs |
| `src/cli/components/thinkingAnimation.tsx` | Uses ShimmerLabel |
| `src/cli/components/customTextInput.tsx` | Uses ShimmerLabel |
| `src/cli/components/inputArea.tsx` | Prop rename |
| `src/cli/components/inputHintsAndStatus.tsx` | Suggestion hint |
| `src/cli/app.tsx` | Esc dismiss, removed typewriter Add IDE selection indicator","body":"### What

Adds a real-time IDE selection indicator to the CLI status bar. When connected to an IDE (e.g., VS Code), the CLI now shows what file and how many lines the user has selected — e.g., 3 lines selected in app.tsx or in app.tsx when the cursor is positioned without a selection. The indicator appears in the bottom hints/status area alongside existing quota info.

  Key changes:

   - New IdeSelectionIndicator component that renders selection context from the connected IDE
   - Wires IDE selection-changed notifications from the MCP host through to React state in app.tsx
   - Positions the indicator on the right side of the status bar; when quota info is present, it moves to a second row to avoid crowding
   - Clears selection state on IDE disconnect
   - Includes unit tests for the new component, updated tests for inputHintsAndStatus, and an E2E test for the IDE MCP integration

<img width="1122" height="265" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6ee16721-7d6a-4fde-9060-78c95de05dd3" />

Goes below the hints and quota 
<img width="1126" height="274" alt="image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ae463376-224c-4730-8812-a95d8576d6b2" />


### Why

When users have the CLI open alongside their IDE, there's no visual feedback about what code they're currently looking at or have selected. This makes it harder to give context-aware prompts — users have to manually describe their selection or copy-paste file references. By surfacing the IDE selection state directly in the CLI, users get immediate awareness of their editing context, making it faster and more natural to ask about or act on the code they're working with.

Fix streaming response size counter for tool call and reasoning deltas","body":"## Overview

### What

Fix the streaming response size counter in the thinking/intent indicator so it updates during tool call and reasoning deltas (or any other streamed chunk that has a size), not just assistant message deltas. Also reset the counter on session idle to avoid showing a stale value between requests.

### Why

The byte counter (`Esc to cancel · X.X KB`) froze whenever the model streamed tool call arguments or reasoning/thinking content, which is the majority of most agent turns.

### Out of scope

This does **not** change the handling for subagents. We have never included their output in the streaming size counter, because they don't emit events for their deltas at all so we don't have that information. Emitting extra events for this would be a more disruptive change.

Enable Content Exclusion FF for staff","body":"## Overview

### What

Enables the content exclusion feature flag for staff rollout, adds logging/telemetry instrumentation for observability, and fixes two bugs discovered during end-to-end testing.

**Bug fixes:**
- **`last_updated_at` schema mismatch**: The zod schema expected `last_updated_at` to be a number (matching the integration guide examples), but the API now returns an ISO 8601 date string. This caused zod validation to reject the entire response, resulting in 0 rules loaded and content exclusion silently not working. Fixed to accept both string and number via `z.union`.
- **Fail-closed on content rule file-read errors**: When a file cannot be read to evaluate content-based exclusion rules (`ifAnyMatch`/`ifNoneMatch`), we now block access instead of allowing it. This matches VS Code behavior — if we cannot verify whether content rules apply, we should protect the file rather than risk exposing excluded content. Added 2 tests covering this case.

**Feature flag:**
- Move `CONTENT_EXCLUSION` from `"off"` to `"staff"`

**Logging:**
- Add info/debug logging across the content exclusion pipeline:
  - Feature flag check (enabled/disabled)
  - Rule fetch results (count of rules and repos)
  - File blocks in str_replace_editor and shell tool (info level)
  - Grep/glob filter counts (debug level)
  - Submodule discovery, cache refresh, and error paths (debug/warning)

**Telemetry:**
- `content_exclusion_rules_fetched` event with `rule_count`, `repo_count`, `submodule_count`, `fetch_duration_ms` metrics — fires on both initial fetch and cache refresh (emitted in both interactive and `-p` modes)
- `content_exclusion_denied` property on tool denied results (flows through `tool_call_executed` events automatically)
- `content_exclusion_filtered_count` metric on grep/glob results when exclusion filtering removes matches

**Log level consistency:**
- Service-level `isExcluded` hit demoted to debug (tools already log at info)
- str_replace_editor block promoted to info (matches shell tool)

### Why

Preparing for staff rollout of content exclusion. We need observability into whether rules are being fetched, how many are active, and when files are being blocked, so we can monitor the feature in production before broader rollout. The `last_updated_at` bug was discovered during live testing against a real repository with content exclusion policies configured — content exclusion was completely non-functional due to the schema mismatch. The fail-closed fix addresses a safety gap where unreadable files with content rules were being allowed through.

Add warning messages for skill loading failures","body":"## Overview

### What

Skills with unknown frontmatter fields now load with warnings instead of being silently skipped. Skills that completely fail validation are shown as errors. This mirrors the behavior introduced for custom agents in PR #2541.

**Key changes:**
- `loadSkills()` now returns `{ skills, warnings, errors }` instead of just `skills[]`
- `parseSkillFile()` and `parseCommandFile()` warn about unknown fields instead of rejecting the skill entirely
- Skills that fail to load entirely (validation failures, missing required fields) are reported as **errors**
- Skills that load but have unknown fields ignored are reported as **warnings**
- `SkillPicker` displays errors (✖ red) and warnings (• yellow) distinctly
- `/skills list` and `/skills reload` commands show errors and warnings in output

### Screenshots

**/skills**:
<img width="2464" height="856" alt="CleanShot 2026-02-03 at 20 40 15@2x" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e4089745-7637-4bd8-9eea-f4ff7a61c7b3" />


**/skills list**
<img width="4022" height="1252" alt="CleanShot 2026-02-03 at 20 40 25@2x" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ed15d52c-48b6-42cd-b71d-2298f0dd0736" />


**/skills reload**
<img width="2400" height="654" alt="CleanShot 2026-02-03 at 20 40 38@2x" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/77a22643-0409-4f03-8c85-2ebc42d03a9e" />

### Why

When skills fail to load due to unsupported frontmatter fields, users previously had no visibility into why their skills were not appearing. This made debugging skill configuration issues difficult. By loading skills anyway and showing warnings, users can:
1. Still use their skills even if they have extra fields
2. See exactly which fields are being ignored
3. Take action to fix their skill definitions proactively

Additionally, distinguishing between errors (skill failed to load) and warnings (skill loaded with some fields ignored) helps users understand the severity of each issue.

Save large pasted content to workspace files","body":"## Overview

### What

When users paste large amounts of text (>30KB) into the CLI input box, automatically save it to a file in the session workspace and display a compact token instead.

**User sees in input box:**
```
❯ Please analyze this [Saved pasted content to workspace (35.2 KB) id=1]
```

**Model receives on submit:**
```
Please analyze this <pasted_content file="/path/to/paste-1706400000.txt" size="35.2 KB" lines="1247" />
```

Key features:
- Uses same 30KB threshold as tool output handling (`DEFAULT_LARGE_OUTPUT_THRESHOLD`)
- Saves to workspace `files/` directory (persistent across session)
- Token is atomic (can't be partially edited - arrow keys skip over it, backspace/delete remove entire token)
- Created shared `useAtomicTokens.ts` utility for consistent token handling (also refactored image slugs to use it)


https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bb38bcec-4dde-4900-ba21-0868416c5631



### Why

Users could shoot themselves in the foot by pasting in arbitrarily large inputs. Now you can paste in whatever you want!

### What

Refactor PRU billing to message-level billing","body":"## Overview

### What

Refactor PRU (Premium Request Unit) billing from turn-based logic to explicit message-level billing metadata. This replaces the hidden state flags (`forcePremiumRequest`) with a cleaner Symbol-based approach where each message can explicitly declare if it's billable.

One detail is that we need/want to use a symbol for tracking this instead of a field, so that the billable state does not leak into logs/events.jsonl/sdk etc. Using a symbol means it will be much harder to accidentally leak this.

**Key changes:**
- Add `src/core/billingMetadata.ts` with Symbol-based billing utilities (`setBillable`, `isBillable`)
- `PremiumRequestProcessor` now checks `isBillable()` on messages instead of turn number
- Remove `setForcePremiumRequest()` flag-based approach from interface and callers
- Billing metadata is cleared after processing to prevent re-billing in subsequent turns
- Update `cloneMessage()` to preserve Symbol-keyed metadata (structuredClone doesn't copy Symbol keys per JS spec)

### Why

The current turn-based billing system is:
1. **Confusing** - Hard to reason about when billing happens
2. **Bug-prone** - State flags can get out of sync
3. **Incomplete** - User steering messages (sent while agent is processing) were billed via side effects

The new message-level approach makes billing explicit and predictable:
- Each message explicitly declares if it's billable
- If ANY message in a model request is billable, charge a PRU
- Billing metadata uses Symbol keys so it's not exposed to SDK or API

Add child custom instructions support for monorepos","body":"## Overview

### What

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e88e26a1-36c0-4eb1-b854-daacefb9d7f9

Add support for discovering and surfacing instruction files from child directories (up to 2 levels deep) in monorepo projects. When the `CHILD_CUSTOM_INSTRUCTIONS` staff feature flag is enabled, the agent scans subdirectories for instruction files (`.github/copilot-instructions.md`, `.copilot-instructions.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `COPILOT.md`) and presents them as references in a markdown table in the system prompt. This allows the agent to read them on-demand when working in relevant areas, without bloating the context window.

Key changes:
- Add `collectChildInstructionFiles()` in `path-helpers.ts` for downward directory traversal
- Add `buildSubdirectoryCustomInstructions()` to render discovered files as a markdown table
- Add `CHILD_CUSTOM_INSTRUCTIONS` staff feature flag gating the feature
- Wire feature flags through `cliSystemMessage()` to all call sites (including `CliAgent`)
- Reuse `DEFAULT_IGNORED_DIRECTORIES` (node_modules, .git, vendor, dist, etc.) for exclusion
- Comprehensive unit and snapshot tests

### Why

In monorepo projects, subdirectories often have their own instruction files with context-specific rules. Without this change, the agent only loads instructions from the repository root, missing important per-package guidelines. This feature enables the agent to be aware of these files and read them when relevant.

result.detailedContent:
{"total_count":25,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":3958495962,"number":3450,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"CLI: Add performance measurement instrumentation with --perf flag","body":"## Overview

### What

Adds a comprehensive performance measurement system for the Copilot CLI, gated behind a hidden `--perf` flag. When enabled, it tracks rendering, event processing, perceived latency, startup, and memory metrics — printing a summary report to stderr on exit.

**New trackers:**
- **RenderPerformanceTracker** — frame count, render duration, CellRenderer diff stats, FPS
- **EventProcessingTracker** — debounce batch sizes, event-to-processing latency, queue depth
- **LatencyTracker** — time-to-first-token (TTFT), time-to-idle (TTI) per turn
- **StartupTracker** — CLI cold-start milestones (modules, session init, first render)
- **MemoryTracker** — labeled memory snapshots at lifecycle points
- **PerfReporter** — aggregates all summaries into a formatted terminal report

**Usage:** `copilot --perf` (combine with `--show-timing` for LLM turn timing too)

**Design:** Zero overhead when `--perf` is not passed — trackers are only instantiated conditionally. Instrumentation hooks into two chokepoints: `CellRenderer.handleFrame()` (all Ink frames) and `useTimeline` debounce pipeline (all session events).

### Why

The CLI has no systematic way to detect rendering regressions, diagnose UI bottlenecks, or quantify performance improvements. This lays the instrumentation foundation for developer diagnostics and future CI regression detection.

### Checklist

data.content:
Good — no PRs found for those. Here's the updated list of bugs **with no linked PRs**:

### 🟢 Simpler
1. **[#3503](https://github.com/github/copilot-agent-runtime/issues/3503)** — LSP client assumes UTF-8 encoding
   - A few `readFileSync("utf-8")` calls in `src/tools/lsp.ts` need encoding detection. Single file.

2. **[#2018](https://github.com/github/copilot-agent-runtime/issues/2018)** — CLI creates files ignoring `.gitattributes` line endings
   - The `create`/`edit` tools need to respect the repo's line ending config.

3. **[#1984](https://github.com/github/copilot-agent-runtime/issues/1984)** — Pressing Escape lingers on "cancelling" for seconds
   - UX fix to make cancel feel more responsive.

### 🟡 Medium
4. **[#1644](https://github.com/github/copilot-agent-runtime/issues/1644)** — Nested ANSI styles break in markdown blockquotes
   - Well-diagnosed with root cause analysis. Touches forked `marked-terminal`.

5. **[#1803](https://github.com/github/copilot-agent-runtime/issues/1803)** — `/share gist` says rate limit exceeded
   - Likely a straightforward API error handling issue.

Any of these catch your eye?

arguments.name: escape-cancel-investigation
arguments.prompt:
Investigate how the Escape key cancellation works in this CLI codebase to determine if there's still a noticeable delay/linger when pressing Escape during an ongoing operation.

Specifically:
1. Find the code that handles Escape key press for cancellation (look in src/cli/components/ and src/cli/hooks/)
2. Find how cancellation is propagated (AbortController, state updates, etc.)
3. Look for any timeouts, delays, or async operations that might cause the UI to show "cancelling" for several seconds before actually stopping
4. Check if there have been any recent changes (git log) to the cancellation flow that might have fixed this
5. Look for any "cancelling" or "Cancelling" text in the UI components

Report what you find about whether this bug likely still exists.
